runners.name: Sovereign Spell
runners.spotlight: Made all for comfortable reappearance success in Wolverhampton maiden (6f, AW) that was won last year by Double Rush, who followed up in this race; mark is unaltered for this second attempt at handicap level; interesting given his good credentials and likelihood of further progress.
runners.comment: Gained reappearance win in AW maiden that threw up the winner of this contest last year

runners.name: Albaydaa
runners.spotlight: Made all in Wolverhampton maiden (7f, AW) to take her 2yo record to 321; likely to be as effective back down in distance; looks the type to improve further and do well in handicaps for her major connections; attractively weighted near the bottom of the card and is one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Looks the type to improve further and do well in handicaps for her major connections

runners.name: Treanmor
runners.spotlight: Cost 2,000,000euros as a yearling and impressively justified favouritism over C&D (good to firm) last May on debut; beaten in three races over 7f/1m since but ran well at Meydan latest; gelded since; interesting with progress possible returned to this scenario on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Impressive over C&D last May and looks interesting back in this scenario on handicap debut

runners.name: Silent Applause
runners.spotlight: Absent since coming up short in Listed grade at York Ebor festival when bidding for 5f hat-trick; return to 6f presents a question mark in this competitive field and William Buick prefers Treanmor; however, cost 1,400,000gns at the Craven breeze-up a year ago and can't be written off.
runners.comment: Has a bit to prove back at 6f but can't be written off; William Buick prefers Treanmor

runners.name: Advertised
runners.spotlight: Appeared to run well in valuable prize at York (7f) on final 2yo start but an 11lb rise in his mark doesn't help in this ongoing bid for a first turf success; 2-2 on AW, both performances at Newcastle (6f/7f).
runners.comment: 11lb rise in his mark in this ongoing bid for a first turf win; 2-2 on AW

runners.name: Sayidah Hard Spun
runners.spotlight: Gained her handicap success at a major track (6f) but against only three rivals and faces a tougher scenario in this deeper field; 4lb rise for final effort (fifth in substandard AW Group 3) adds to the difficulty; on a positive note, there is a chance she can still improve further.
runners.comment: Raised 4lb for finishing fifth in substandard AW Group 3 but may do better still

runners.name: Reciprocated
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since disappointing in Ayr nursery in September when back down in class after defeats at Listed/Group level; commenced last season with two wins over 6f, looking a useful sort, and this reappearance may be the time to catch him; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Commenced last season with two wins; this reappearance may be the time to catch him
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Lebron Power
runners.spotlight: Brings very good stats, being 3-6 overall, 2-2 in handicaps (both Class 4) and 2-2 over 6f; prevailed narrowly at Newbury (soft) on last appearance; now 2lb higher in a better race but may have further improvement in her; one to consider.
runners.comment: Brings good stats, being 3-6 overall, 2-2 in handicaps and 2-2 over 6f; one to consider

runners.name: Enricher
runners.spotlight: Required a switch to AW at Wolverhampton (6f maiden) to open his account at the fourth attempt, and needs further improvement to follow up in this warm handicap back on turf; wears first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Needed a switch to AW to open his account; goes into deeper waters back on turf

runners.name: Song Of The Clyde
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in the Two Year Old Trophy at Redcar when last seen but otherwise a progressive sort and may do better still; performed well in sales races at York (won) and Doncaster (close second; form has been strongly franked); capable of playing a big role, even off top weight.
runners.comment: Largely progressive form includes good performances in two sales races; in the mix
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker

runners.name: Ironwill
runners.spotlight: Built on debut effort by completing a September double, showing versatility with the wins over 6f on fast ground and 5f on soft; readily defied a penalty in novice event at Beverley and looks open to further progress this season; possibilities.
runners.comment: Completed a September double; looks open to further progress this term; possibilities

runners.name: Pilu
runners.spotlight: Absent since finishing last of four in Salisbury conditions race when attempting to take record to 3-3; may still have more to offer but RPRs suggest he's facing a stiff task off a mark of 87 in this deep field.
runners.comment: Proved unable to take record to 3-3 and RPRs suggests he's on a stiff mark

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Had an easy task for sole win; good second in sales/Listed races at York, including behind Song Of The Clyde, but form dipped sharply on final 2yo start; has a difficult mark in this first handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Ran well in one of his Listed attempts but has to overcome a difficult mark

runners.name: Sir Alfie
runners.spotlight: Scored twice during his spell with Ger Lyons, then posted duck eggs in two Listed events for Charles Byrnes; gelded since; 1-1 at handicap level but this looks a tougher task on debut for new stable.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; 1-1 at handicap level but this looks a stiffer task on stable debut

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Good seventh of 19 at the York Ebor festival (6f) in sole handicap attempt but is a tad exposed, as record of 0-6 since debut success reflects; below-par third of four in conditions race on final 2yo start.
runners.comment: Tad exposed, as record of 0-6 since debut success reflects; below par last time

runners.name: Rogue Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Reasonably exposed and doesn't look the answer on the balance of his AW form this year; gained his 2yo wins at a lower level; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Returns to turf; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear

races.race_name: Betway Wood Ditton Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crown Knott
runners.spotlight: Already-gelded son of Lope De Vega; third foal; half-brother to 5f winner Mc Loven (including 2yo/AW; RPR 88); dam 6f 2yo winner (81), half-sister to winners Blair House (UAE 1m1f Group 1) and Key Victory (1m2f Listed), out of maiden sister to 1m Group 1 winner Poet's Voice; represents powerful connections who have fielded winners of this race before; key contender.
runners.comment: Already-gelded son of Lope De Vega; powerful connections and he's a key contender

runners.name: Portcullis
runners.spotlight: By Frankel; second foal; half-brother to 1m winner Crown Estate (including AW; RPR 100); dam 1m winner (including AW and French 1,000 Guineas; 112), half-sister to 7f Listed winner Top Score, out of unraced sister to this yard's top-class 7f-1m2f winner Raven's Pass; plenty of blue blood in that pedigree and he's one of two for a stable that won this in 2021 and 2022; of obvious interest on this debut with Ryan Moore booked.
runners.comment: By Frankel out of French 1,000 Guineas winner; of obvious interest with Ryan Moore booked
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: North Beach
runners.spotlight: 390,000euros yearling by New Bay; third foal; half-brother to 1m AW winner Indian Spirit (RPR 86); dam Listed-placed 1m2f winner (96), half-sister to winners Sabbeeh (smart 6f-1m) and Dream Day (useful 6f 2yo), out of 7f Listed winner/1,000 Guineas fourth; the most expensive of these at auction and hails from a leading yard that won this with a well-backed colt last year; betting should prove informative again.
runners.comment: 390,000euros yearling by New Bay; leading yard won this with well-backed colt last year

runners.name: Santushti
runners.spotlight: Dubawi fifth foal; brother to winners Lord Of Love (1m2f/1m4f; RPR 102) and Governor Of India (1m2f AW; 77); dam 1m-10.5f winner (including 2yo/French Oaks; 109), closely related to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner English Ballet, half-sister to 1m Listed winner King Of Comedy; lots to like about that pedigree and it will be interesting to see what the market makes of him, particularly in relation to his top stable's other contender Portcullis; firmly considered with Oisin Murphy up.
runners.comment: By Dubawi out of a French Oaks winner; must be considered with Oisin Murphy on board

runners.name: Me An' All
runners.spotlight: Dream Ahead sixth foal; half-sister to four winners including Ventura Gold (7f-1m1f including AW; RPR 79), Chloe's Courage (7f AW; 56) and Kannon (French 5.5f/7.5f); dam 7f AW winner (76), out of 11.7f winning sister to 7f Group 3 winner Yalaitanee; the sole filly and best watched without good market support.
runners.comment: The sole filly and she's best watched without good market support

runners.name: Bemersyde
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns yearling by Ghaiyyath; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Warnie (6f-1m including 2yo/Australian Group 2; RPR 115), Montpellier Green (7f 2yo; 81) and Mutaraafeq (1m AW; 79); dam French 9.7f AW winner (including 2yo), half-sister to 1m1f Listed winner On Call Now; respected connections and market may help ahead of this introduction.
runners.comment: 120,000gns yearling by Ghaiyyath; market may help ahead of this introduction

runners.name: Cosmo Brown
runners.spotlight: By Ghaiyyath; 34,000euros foal, 25,000gns yearling; second foal; half-brother to German 1m2f winner Poyraz Approach; dam unraced sister to useful winners News At Six (1m2f-1m4f) and Leafy Shade (6f 2yo/8.5f); likely best watched on this first go unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 34,000euros foal, 25,000gns yearling; best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise

runners.name: Dark Whisper
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling by Dark Angel; fourth foal; half-brother to German 1m1f AW winner Bavaria; dam 9.4f-1m4f winner (including AW/Listed; RPR 102), sister to unraced dam of 6f Group 1 winner Shaquille, out of 5f Group 2 winner; mixed messages distance-wise from this gelding's pedigree but he's worth a market check.
runners.comment: 50,000euros yearling; mixed messages distance-wise from this gelding's pedigree

runners.name: Impierious
runners.spotlight: By Palace Pier; tenth foal; closely related to 1m-1m4f winner Mashhoor (including Group 3; RPR 115), half-brother to seven winners including Fambrus (Italian 1m7f Listed; 101) and Faciascura (Italian 1m2f Group 3; 100); dam Italian Group-placed 1m2f/1m3f winner; yard in decent nick but this gelding's claims would be aided by market support.
runners.comment: By Palace Pier; yard in decent nick but gelding's claims would be aided by market support
runners.owner: Hasmonean Racing, S Hope & S Barrow

runners.name: Take A Chill Pill
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling by Kingman; second foal; half-brother to 1m2f AW winner Star Cast (RPR 71); dam Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (including 2yo/AW; 105), is granddaughter of 11.7f winning sister to 7f Group 3 winner Yalaitanee; the only one of these to hold a big-race entry (The Derby) so look for market support to back that up.
runners.comment: 65,000gns yearling by Kingman; holds a Derby entry; look for market support

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who held on by a neck in last year's Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm); had excuses for her subsequent defeats in other major sprints at Goodwood (drop back to 5f; still ran creditably) and Haydock (poorly drawn); has the best chance at the weights.
runners.comment: Won last year's Commonwealth Cup; subsequent defeats can be excused; leading contender
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Beckford's Folly
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to prevailing narrowly in the Cornwallis (5f, good; Group 3) on final 2yo start, taking record on the Newmarket tracks to 3-3; effective over 6f and looks open to further improvement; carries 3lb penalty; respected kept to this venue in first attempt against his elders.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to Cornwallis win, taking record on the Newmarket tracks to 3-3; respected

runners.name: Elmonjed
runners.spotlight: Has form on the Newmarket tracks but recorded last three wins at York, including when beating Crestofdistinction with more in hand than the narrow margin suggests in Listed grade on most recent appearance; likely to be more interesting when back at that venue.
runners.comment: Recorded last three wins at York and may be more interesting when back there

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive, mostly in handicaps, throughout his career; won by 1l going away from Jasour in Listed event at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) on seasonal debut, looking better than ever and taking record to 6-23; solid second in both C&D starts; enters calculations upped further in grade.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; won by 1l going away from Jasour at Doncaster on seasonal debut

runners.name: Prince Of India
runners.spotlight: Only fifth when favourite for AW event on reappearance but is reasonably interesting back on turf, provided he gets ideal ground; has form figures of 12113 on good/firmer, the final effort in Listed grade behind Elmonjed; may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 12113 on good/firmer; interesting provided he gets similar ground

runners.name: Diligent Harry
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Listed sprint double at Lingfield this year, sharing the spoils with Completely Random on reappearance; similar to that rival, looks far from guaranteed to maintain the form back on turf; 0-18 in this sphere.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Lingfield Listed double this year; 0-18 on turf

runners.name: My Mate Alfie
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding whose good spell in 2024 featured a Group 3 win and who ran creditably a few times last year; seemed to need the run at Doncaster on stable/seasonal debut but has work to do to reverse placings with Aramram and Jasour.
runners.comment: Group winner in Ireland; seemed to need the run at Doncaster on debut for new yard

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Very useful front-runner whose strike-rate is 12-32; bagged Group 3 honours at York (7f) last June and a valuable pot at Doha (6f) most recently; unbeaten (4-4) in 6f contests on the Newmarket courses and has shown that he can win in sizeable fields; likely to make a bold bid back here.
runners.comment: Very useful front-runner; unbeaten (4-4) on the Newmarket courses; should make a bold bid

runners.name: Completely Random
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated with Diligent Harry in Listed grade to complete a Lingfield 6f double in January and take AW record to 4-8; effective when fresh but is by no means certain to transfer the improvement back to turf; only 1-9 in this sphere; tongue-tie applied.
runners.comment: Dead-heated with Diligent Harry in Listed race, taking AW record to 4-8; just 1-9 on turf

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 5f last year, bagging Listed honours at York in July prior to facing stiffer tasks returned to Group 1 level; effective at 6f in the past and produced his peak RPR when landing this prize (on good ground) in 2024; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 5f Listed winner in 2025; produced peak RPR when landing this prize in 2024; not ruled out

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Very useful 6f performer who registered last success in Group 3 contest at Ascot two years ago; has Group 1 form, including creditable sixth in the 2025 July Cup; made a solid start to his 2026 campaign with 1l second to Aramram at Doncaster; again likely to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Very useful and has Group 1 form; solid effort behind Aramram at Doncaster on reappearance

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Proved well ahead of his mark in sole handicap attempt and only start over 6f, then finished a good third in the Cornwallis (behind Beckford's Folly) and the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; receives weight all round and could go well.
runners.comment: Good third in the Cornwallis and Breeders' Cup race; 1-1 over 6f; could go well

runners.name: Crestofdistinction
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-5 on turf compared to 2-3 on AW; admittedly registered his peak RPR in this sphere when second Elmonjed in York Listed contest (6f) on penultimate appearance; however, needs an even bigger effort to go one better in this field.
runners.comment: Posted peak RPR on turf but needs an even bigger effort to gain first win in this sphere

runners.name: Run To Freedom
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form, such as placed efforts in two July Cups, but is getting long in the tooth and seems unlikely to break his Abernant duck at the fourth attempt (record in this race reads 404); losing spell goes back almost three years.
runners.comment: Capable of smart form but seems unlikely to break his Abernant duck at the fourth attempt

runners.name: Hawk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Made all and did enough to beat two stablemates in the Futurity at Doncaster (heavy; Group 1) to complete a 1m hat-trick and take record to 3-4; may ideally need further this year, especially when the ground isn't testing; otherwise the form pick and an improving Ballydoyle colt to boot.
runners.comment: Made all in the Futurity to complete a 1m hat-trick; improving colt who is the form pick
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Hidden Force
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who is two from two, namely AW wins at Kempton, last time comfortably beating Venetian Prince in 1m conditions race that his yard landed in 2024 and 2025 with subsequent Group 1 scorers (including 2,000 Guineas); the signs look very good for this highly promising sort.
runners.comment: Two from two, namely AW wins at Kempton, latest in a notable conditions race; respected

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Unsuited by heavy ground in the Futurity on final 2yo start; steadily progressive otherwise, and ran well in the Champagne Stakes at Doncaster and Dewhurst at Newmarket; however, chance of opening his Group account largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of opening his Group account depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Scored at Newbury when last seen on turf; looked flattered to finished just under 2l behind Hidden Force, who had something in hand, at Kempton most recently and is unlikely to reverse the placings; however, bred to do better still and may produce another good effort.
runners.comment: Unlikely to reverse Kempton placings with Hidden Force but again could go well

runners.name: Commander's Intent
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-7 and broadly consistent last term; creditable second in last three starts, including in 7f Group 3 at Newmarket; however, his ceiling is seemingly already known and, unlike all of today's rivals, he doesn't have a 2,000 Guineas entry.
runners.comment: His ceiling is seemingly already known and he doesn't have a Guineas entry

runners.name: Hankelow
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder colt who nearly went 3-3 last season, the sole defeat when nabbed by Avicenna on the line at Doncaster; justified favouritism in the Autumn Stakes over C&D (good to firm; Group 3) next time, making all; progressive and holds solid claims.
runners.comment: Made all in Group 3 over C&D on last appearance; progressive and has solid claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Two from two last year, both starts over 7f at Doncaster, latest in Listed grade (on good ground) when collaring Hankelow on the line despite still seeming green; may have plenty of further progress in him, especially over this extra furlong; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Two from two, both starts at Doncaster; collared Hankelow in Listed grade latest

runners.name: Baandee
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three starts, all over this trip at Chelmsford (AW); doesn't look to be doing anything wrong in a finish and sets the standard on form; contender again with nothing in her pedigree suggesting this switch to turf will be a problem.
runners.comment: Runner-up all three starts (7f, AW) and she sets the standard; turf debut

runners.name: Pointe Bleu
runners.spotlight: Blue Point fifth foal; half-sister to winners Yaanaas (1m AW; RPR 99) and Orionis (1m3f-1m4f; 97); dam French Listed-placed 9.5f/1m4f winner (99), sister to 1m2f/10.5f Group 1 winner Almanzor; market will reveal more but she's a likely contender for leading connections.
runners.comment: Blue Point filly from family of star colt Almanzor; key debutante for leading connections

runners.name: Hassaleh
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder filly; dam 6f and 7f (Group 2) 2yo winner/1,000 Guineas second (116); 13-2 when encouraging third of nine in 6f Windsor fillies' novice (good) in June; not seen since but almost certainly has best days ahead of her and any market confidence could prove telling.
runners.comment: Night Of Thunder filly; 3rd in 6f Windsor fillies' novice (good) in June; player on return

runners.name: Eloquencia
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; twelfth foal; half-sister to seven winners including Pimento (Group-placed 1m AW including 2yo; RPR 112), White Bullet (6f AW; 87), Zakreet (7f/1m 2yo; 86) and Valkyrja (1m; 71); dam unraced sister to 11.4f Listed winner Birdie; market might help ahead of this debut.
runners.comment: 52,000gns yearling by Sea The Stars; market might help ahead of this debut

runners.name: La Mariposa
runners.spotlight: By Zoustar; half-sister to 6f AW winner Lord Danielson (RPR 57); dam 7f/1m winner (including AW; 93), sister to UAE 1m winner Pictograph, out of unraced half-sister to 1m2f-1m4f Group 1 winner Vengeance Of Rain; stable not a noted source of winning newcomers but worth a market check.
runners.comment: By Zoustar; dam 7f/1m winner (93); unlikely newcomer but market check advised
runners.owner: L J Vaessen And Stephen Barber

runners.name: Thursday Girl
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns foal by Pinatubo; looked raw but plugged on late when fifth of nine in Salisbury maiden (7f, good) in October; yard among the winners and she's surely capable of better.
runners.comment: 240,000gns foal by Pinatubo; raw when fifth on sole start last year; better on the cards

races.race_name: Betway EBF "Confined" Novice Stakes (For horses Which Have Run No More Than Once) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Maho Bay
runners.spotlight: By Dubawi; third foal; half-brother to winners Cavallo Bay (Group-placed 7f 2yo; RPR 101) and Spring Beauty (French 5.5f); dam 6f/1m 2yo winner (including Canadian turf Grade 1; 106); 5-6 for five-runner maiden at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in December, leading 2f out for a 6l win; that came with great authority, so this Dante and Derby entry looks a good prospect.
runners.comment: Kempton win (1m3f, AW; 5-6) in December came with great authority; Dante and Derby entry

runners.name: Guildmaster
runners.spotlight: 325,000gns yearling by Teofilo; ninth foal; brother to four winners including Group winners Guaranteed (RPR 109) and Gear Up (114); 13-8 favourite for novice at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and came from fifth of 12 at halfway to win by  3l, despite looking green; Dante and Derby entry from a top yard, who'll surely stay and find plenty of improvement this term.
runners.comment: Fav, 3l win at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December despite looking green; Dante and Derby entry

runners.name: Galiyan
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns yearling by Galiway; fifth foal; closely related to 1m1f-1m2f winner Integrant (including AW Listed; RPR 108), half-brother to French 9.5f winner Viatore; dam Group-placed 7f/1m 2yo winner (103), half-sister to Group 3 winners at 7f and 1m; trainer won this last year with another Dante (and Derby) entry and this debutant needs a close look.
runners.comment: 180,000gns yearling by Galiway; Dante entry, so needs a close look despite debut

runners.name: Amadeus Mozart
runners.spotlight: By Wootton Bassett out of a Galileo mare; 10-11 for maiden at the Curragh (7f, good) last June and always prominent to win that ten-runner race by half a length in a five-way scrap, but he has not raced since; stablemate in second was useful afterwards but the third showed no better than fair form; a promising Dante and Derby entry, for whom the market may guide.
runners.comment: 10-11, won Curragh maiden (7f, good) last June in a five-way scrap; Dante and Derby entry
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Birgham Dub
runners.spotlight: 240,000euros yearling; by Sea The Moon out of a close relation to 1m2f Group 1 winner Sultanina; second favourite for seven-runner maiden at Haydock (7f, good) last July, which he nearly blew when making an awkward start and first off the bridle but he came from the back and stuck his neck out well for a narrow win; absent since but should be open to major improvement.
runners.comment: Green when winning narrowly from out the back in a maiden at Haydock (7f, good) last July

runners.name: Sir Griflet
runners.spotlight: Camelot half-brother to five winners, notably Bolshoi Ballet (1m-1m4f including US Grade 1; RPR 119); 46l defeat at Southwell (1m3f, AW; 14-1) five days ago suggests he faces a very stiff task.
runners.comment: 46l defeat at Southwell (1m3f, AW; 14-1) five days ago suggests he faces a very stiff task

races.race_name: Watch Live Racing At Betway Handicap

runners.name: Storm Free
runners.spotlight: Good-ground maiden winner on his April return last year and it was a good run after a break when third of 14 at Newcastle (also 6f, AW) in August; signed off by beating 14 others at Leicester (7f, good) in October after which he was gelded; retains plenty of potential ahead of this comeback on just his fourth handicap start.
runners.comment: Won in big field; plenty of potential ahead of this comeback on just fourth handicap start

runners.name: Eminency
runners.spotlight: Kept fairly busy in the last 12 months and he supplemented a 6f Windsor (good) win in September by scoring over 7f at Yarmouth (good to soft) in October and at Chelmsford (AW) in December; respectable efforts on both starts last month and Oisin Murphy takes over for the first time.
runners.comment: Respectable on both starts last month and Oisin Murphy takes over for the first time

runners.name: Morte Point
runners.spotlight: 3-4 since asked to tackle 7f from late August with wins at Newbury (good) and twice on AW (latterly dead-heating); big fields no problem and he can likely do better again this term; player if tuned up.
runners.comment: 3-4 since asked to tackle 7f; big fields no problem; can likely do better again this term

runners.name: Goldmoyne
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who has been productive on AW in cheekpieces this winter for James Owen; went back-to-back at Newcastle (6f) in December and secured a four-timer when scoring at Lingfield (1m; first three legs at 7f) last month; creditable third back on turf (7f, good to soft) last time and his two wins on turf in Ireland came in big fields on a sound surface; considered.
runners.comment: Prolific on AW for new yard and two wins in Ireland came in big fields on a sound surface

runners.name: Bold Impact
runners.spotlight: First-time blinkers when making all for easy win in Epsom novice (7f, soft) in September 2024; encouraging return from a year's absence the following October but he left Ralph Beckett after a moderate effort next time; weak in the market for his stable debut when it all went wrong at the start 19 days ago.
runners.comment: Weak in the market for his stable debut when it all went wrong at the start 19 days ago

runners.name: Royal Velvet
runners.spotlight: Steady improver who scored three times last year, including here (1m) in May and on the July course (7f, also good to firm) two months later; also landed valuable big-field fillies' handicap at York which bodes well for this test; should be ready to roll on this return and she's much respected.
runners.comment: Steady improver who scored three times last year; should be ready and she's much respected

runners.name: Physique
runners.spotlight: C&D novice winner back in the day and he was trained by Micky Fenton in Ireland when landing big Galway handicap (7f, heavy) in 2024; back with his former trainer (in Newmarket this time) when weakening over 6f at Doncaster 18 days ago; might step forward from that comeback.
runners.comment: Big Galway handicap win for this trainer in 2024; might step forward from recent comeback

runners.name: Gangsta Man
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f (good/good to yielding) winner in Ireland who was soon outpaced before coming home strongly to be beaten a whisker in 5f Scottish Sprint Cup (soft) 12 days ago; that was a really encouraging first go for Jim Goldie and this step back up to 7f should be fine; has to be of interest.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner; big effort over 5f on recent stable debut; has to be of interest

runners.name: Dapper Guest
runners.spotlight: Improved in headgear over 7f last term, winning in first-time cheekpieces on the July course (good to firm) in June and in a newly fitted visor at Southwell in August; also scored in latter aid at Chelmsford (also AW) in October; slight worry slow starts were a feature of his last two runs but might have needed a rest by then; not ruled out on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Improved in headgear over 7f last term (three wins); not ruled out on this reappearance

runners.name: Sujet
runners.spotlight: Won at the Curragh (1m1f) on seasonal/handicap debut in June and doubled his tally in Killarney conditions event (1m, also good) in August; big disappointment on final start for Dermot Weld in late September but new connections had to go to 150,000gns to secure him one month later; unexposed and interesting to see how he goes in the market ahead of this first start at 7f.
runners.comment: Won twice for Dermot Weld; left that yard for 150,000gns; first try at 7f and unexposed

runners.name: Huscal
runners.spotlight: Two wins as a juvenile (6f, good/good to firm); couldn't add to that tally last year but he was gelded in May and went close three times at 7f (good to firm/AW) subsequently; returns on a competitive-looking mark and he's considered.
runners.comment: Went close three times at 7f last term; returns on a competitive-looking mark

runners.name: Havana Pusey
runners.spotlight: Progressed last term; pick of her efforts came when fourth in AW Group 3 (6f) in June and when beating all bar Royal Velvet in July course handicap (7f, good to firm) the following month; will need to be near her best to make a winning seasonal debut but was beaten only a nose on last year's return.
runners.comment: Needs to be near best to make a winning comeback but beaten a nose on last year's return

runners.name: Dragon Leader
runners.spotlight: Yet to match his 2yo best (included big sales race and Listed wins) but he's only had eight subsequent starts and latest third at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) four weeks ago was a firm step back in the right direction; same mark and there's a suspicion he could be on the way back; player if so.
runners.comment: Good 2yo and latest third was encouraging; suspicion he could be on the way back

runners.name: Percy's Lad
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this race in 2024 when it was staged as a Class 2; made all at Chester (7.5f, good to firm) on his next start and it was a solid fourth back there (7f, good to soft) when last seen 20 months ago; yard going well and handicapper has given him a shot too; watch the market.
runners.comment: Absent 20 months but h'capper has given him a shot and yard going well; watch the market

runners.name: God Of War
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground Sandown winner in August who is just 1lb higher; comes here fit after two respectable fourth-place finishes at Kempton (also 7f, AW); acts on this forecast good ground and could make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Just 1lb above last win and fit from AW; could make his presence felt

runners.name: Jordan Electrics
runners.spotlight: Admirable old boy who had a sparkling 2024, winning seven times (5f/6f, sound surface) mostly at Hamilton; couldn't add to that haul last year, though, and never figured when last on his recent reappearance (soft ground admittedly against him); tries 7f for the first time on this 84th start.
runners.comment: Admirable old boy but no win last year and return was poor; tries 7f for the first time
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Crimson Spirit
runners.spotlight: Started last term very well; third of 21 at York (good) in May followed by hat-trick of wins (also 7f, good to firm) across June and July; remains 4lb above his last winning mark but could feature if returning in similar vein.
runners.comment: Started last term well; above last winning mark but can feature if returning in same vein

runners.name: Jumby
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who could only beat one home in this race when it was staged as a Class 2 last year; managed a handful of creditable efforts subsequently but it's hard to ignore his last win came back in June 2023 (7f Group 3); returns on reduced mark.
runners.comment: Hard to ignore his last win came back in June 2023 (7f G3) but returns on reduced mark

runners.name: Fly Away Home
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts in maiden hurdles, notably place claims when unseating at the last over this trip at Leopardstown and most recently staying on second in 2m4f Navan maiden last month (soft); return to this trip to suit and leading claims in an ordinary maiden.
runners.comment: Staying on second in 2m4f Navan maiden last month; leading claims in an ordinary maiden

runners.name: Louie's Folly
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat maiden in Britain for James Horton; shaped okay on all three maiden hurdle runs over 2m, notably latest when (favourite) chasing home a ready winner here last month (soft); tries a longer trip here.
runners.comment: Chased home a ready winner here last month (soft); tries a longer trip

runners.name: Doing It In Style
runners.spotlight: Initial promise in maiden hurdles but hasn't progressed, well held on handicap debut last month; looking exposed but can't be ruled out in an ordinary maiden.
runners.comment: Initial promise in maiden hurdles but hasn't progressed; looking exposed

runners.name: Meghan Mala
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam bumper/2m4f hurdle winner, sister to 3m1f chase winner Kalabaloo; 20-1, promising debut in 2m Gowran mares' maiden last month, staying-on third of 10 behind an odds-on shot (heavy); step up in trip here could suit and could well improve enough to feature.
runners.comment: Debut promise last month over 2m; likely improver upped in trip and one to consider

runners.name: Kisuton Enki
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros 3yo; half-sister to four winners inc Yala Enki (high-class 2m1f-3m4f hurdle/chase); dam French maiden jumper; second favourite, reportedly lame when soundly beaten on debut in good-class 2m3f Punchestown mares' bumper in December 2024; respected if strong in the market on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Lame on debut in bumper a while ago; respected if strong in the market on hurdles debut

runners.name: Ma Lumiere
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to bumper and useful 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner A Different Kind; 28-1, never sighted on last month's debut in Cork bumper; best watched now switched to hurdles.
runners.comment: Never sighted on last month's debut in Cork bumper; best watched now switched to hurdles

runners.name: Babyburn
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to seven winners, notably same yard's high-class bumper winner and 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Ballyburn; cost 92,000euros as a 3yo; too keen, soundly beaten on debut in Fairyhouse fillies' bumper in December; switches to hurdles now.
runners.comment: Ballyburn's half-sister too keen, soundly beaten in Fairyhouse bumper; switches to hurdles

runners.name: Little Neck Road
runners.spotlight: Sister to a bumper winner and from the family of smart staying chaser Tullymurry Toff; not without market support but pulled up in a Bartlemy point-to-point in May last year; market likely best guide on rules debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up in sole point; market likely best guide on rules debut
runners.owner: Emma Sheahan & E Fox & E Sheahan

runners.name: Dceilingcantholdus
runners.spotlight: Placed in her two point-to-points last month, remote last of four on rules debut early this month in Cork maiden hunters chase (keen, mistakes); switches to hurdles and best watched.
runners.comment: Remote last early this month in Cork hunters chase (keen, mistakes); switches to hurdles

runners.name: Jessie Roe
runners.spotlight: Four bumper efforts have amounted to little and well held in Rathcannon point-to-point four days ago; can only be watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Four bumper efforts have amounted to little and well held in Rathcannon point' 4 days ago

runners.name: Chosen Princess
runners.spotlight: Failed to land a blow on debut at Thurles in December; more encouraging hurdles debut this month, staying on late after some novicey jumping when remote fourth in 2m3f Cork mares' maiden (yielding); more needed.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging recent hurdles debut; more needed and more fluency needed in jumping

runners.name: Breeze Of Tobruk
runners.spotlight: Ocovango filly; dam point winner/third in bumper, half-sister to 2m hurdle winner Shanroe Society; never sighted (tailed off) on recent debut in 2m1f Cork maiden; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Never sighted (tailed off) on recent debut in 2m1f Cork maiden; can only be watched

runners.name: Collaborative
runners.spotlight: Won Navan handicap hurdle (2m4f, soft) in January of last year off 102 before following up in Fairyhouse novice (2m, heavy); moderate seasonal return at Punchestown but pair of runner-up finishes followed at 2m4f/3m; fair fourth at Naas (15.5f) on latest; solid chance in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Trip-versatile and enjoys the mud; went close twice in December, including around here

runners.name: Ma Jacks Hill
runners.spotlight: Point winner; 0-4 in bumpers; improved for a tongue-tie over hurdles until dropping out very tamely in Navan Grade 3; the form of his second at Galway reads well; not beaten far on handicap debut at Leopardstown; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Point/hurdles winner; Galway second reads well; not beaten far on handicap debut; player

runners.name: Jay Jay Zee
runners.spotlight: Won Ballinrobe maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott on debut in April 2023 but not seen since down the field in Galway handicap 901 days ago; first run for new yard; best watched.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe winner for Gordon Elliott; off the track 901 days; new yard; best watched

runners.name: Miss Corby
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden winner last season (2m1f, good); well held twice since over around 2m4f, latest after winter break in Gowran handicap in February on heavy; ground looks against her here.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe maiden winner last season (2m1f, good); struggled over 2m4f since; prefers good
runners.owner: Mrs T Hyde & Mrs Paul Shanahan

runners.name: Way Down South
runners.spotlight: Won course maiden hurdle in 2024; got off the mark over fences in Cork beginners' in January in first-time headgear combination; still in with a shout when unseating rider six out in handicap chase here 16 days ago; conditions suitable; chance.
runners.comment: Course winner won over fences at Cork in January; fell six out here over fences on latest

runners.name: Colleen Danu
runners.spotlight: Gained second hurdles win at Clonmel (19.5f, good) last May off 101; went close there the following month when 1.25l fourth; was last seen finishing second at Clonmel again in October; best form is on a sounder surface and might need the run.
runners.comment: Some good hurdles form; best form is on a sounder surface and might need the run

runners.name: Crowsatedappletart
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner over hurdles/fences and hasn't been out of the first three in his last six starts around here; three wins over fences last year; disappointing favourite over hurdles at Cork but returns from a break racing from 14lb lower hurdles mark; should go well.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner and hasn't been out of the first 3 in his last six starts around here

runners.name: Grange Walk
runners.spotlight: Last year's winner off 1lb lower but hasn't won since; below his best chasing this season, albeit set some stiff tasks; not discounted back hurdling but veteran will need to improve significantly.
runners.comment: Last year's winner off 1lb lower; poor chasing this season; not discounted back hurdling

runners.name: He Can't Dance
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; useful form in six hurdling starts, never out of the first three; third of 21 in Listed DRF handicap in February (3m, heavy); runner-up in 2m4f Fairyhouse maiden hurdle 12 days ago; will go well again but is becoming costly to follow.
runners.comment: Never out of first three in six hurdles starts; will go well again but becoming costly

runners.name: King's Bucks
runners.spotlight: Point winner; only second run over hurdles when third behind No Drama This End (runner-up also a class act) in Cheltenham Grade 2 in November (2m5f, soft); defeated at odds-on in very testing conditions in course maiden; pulled up in the Albert Bartlet; rated 130 and one to beat.
runners.comment: Classy form at Cheltenham before poor run here when odds-on; pulled up at the Festival

runners.name: Casual Water
runners.spotlight: Mastercraftsman gelding; 150,000euros yearling; half-brother to dual Champion Chase winner Altior; dam bumper/2m1f hurdle winner; will need to be above-average to win this on debut but he might well be.
runners.comment: 150,000euros yearling; half-brother to top-class dual Champion Chase winner Altior

runners.name: Loyal Praetorian
runners.spotlight: Hasn't really fired since finishing second in Clonmel maiden hurdle in December; too much to find on ratings.
runners.comment: Hasn't fired since finishing second in Clonmel maiden hurdle in December; too much to find

runners.name: Tradonthebay
runners.spotlight: Modest third in Gowran bumper (2m, soft to heavy) in January 2025; tailed off here after absence on hurdles debut in Februray; likely to fall well short.
runners.comment: Hinted at some ability but modest form all the same; likely to come up well short

runners.name: Strand Street Lass
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in pair of point-to-points; beaten out of sight in four hurdle starts; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Pulled up in pair of point-to-points; beaten out of sight in four hurdle starts

runners.name: Three County Star
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden after eight starts and can't be fancied taking on far superior opposition.
runners.comment: Modest maiden after eight starts and can't be fancied taking on far superior opposition
runners.owner: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey

runners.name: Sam's Favourite
runners.spotlight: Refused at the second on debut at Leopardstown; triple-figure odds and never a factor in three starts since; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Refused on debut at Leopardstown; triple-figure odds and never a factor in 3 starts since

runners.name: Arctic Flame
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Down Royal maiden last summer (2m1f, good) and better form when second in 2m Gowran handicap on soft; after a break didn't count in very valuable Leopardstown mares' handicap at the DRF in February; this is less competitive so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Didn't count in Leopardstown mares' handicap at DRF; this less competitive

runners.name: Piccolo Player
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden hurdle winner (heavy) in November; has run well in a couple of handicaps although too keen and didn't get home on latest at Gowran; not one to rule out, providing he settles better (tongue-tie added).
runners.comment: Too keen at Gowran last time; tongue-tie back on and previous C&D winner; claims

runners.name: Hardy Stuff
runners.spotlight: Hooded since looking tricky on his debut, showing decent turn of foot to win Leopardstown maiden at Christmas but soundly beaten both starts since in competitive Cheltenham contests, too keen and hung right first occasion and then never sighted in Fred Winter at the Festival; down in grade here.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden winner well held at Cheltenham twice since; drops in grade now

runners.name: Sense Of Occasion
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in Belclare maiden point-to-point and nice bit of improvement on second maiden hurdle run when C&D fifth (heavy) at Christmas; unable to build on that at Down Royal following month when fourth (second and third won afterwards) but still enters calculations on handicap debut.
runners.comment: C&D maiden run at Christmas encouraging; respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Market Ready
runners.spotlight: Okay runs in a couple of maidens and nice bit of improvement on recent handicap debut at Clonmel when good second of 13 (beaten 1l) on soft, only outstayed late; 3lb higher but entitled to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Recent Clonmel second puts him right in the mix despite 3lb rise

runners.name: Shellrunforbriggs
runners.spotlight: Naas maiden winner was well held in handicap next time and has switched to chasing both starts since, chasing home a clear winner in 2m6f Thurles Listed mares' novice in February; interesting back hurdling here
runners.comment: Recent chase second augurs well though ideally wants further

runners.name: Chosen Shant
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner of modest handicap hurdles at Wexford last summer; not nearly as effective a chaser, okay effort when last seen at Thurles in November; wants better ground so likely best watched on return.
runners.comment: Wexford winner last summer on better ground; lacks a recent run and ground a concern

runners.name: Wild Wild Wind
runners.spotlight: Posted several decent efforts in defeat over the winter including over C&D; solid enough Clonmel effort early this month so remains of interest.
runners.comment: C&D second over the winter; recent Clonmel run a solid one; player

runners.name: Barryroe Madam
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 20 starts for Brian McMahon came in 3m Listowel handicap last May (good); ground probably too soft when last seen in November and ground concerns here on yard debut; trip on the short side too.
runners.comment: Best form at 3m on better ground so possibly best watched on yard debut after winter break

runners.name: Sidiriya
runners.spotlight: First reserve; Flat winner in 2022; nowhere near as effective a hurdler but penultimate Naas third (2m, soft to heavy) offers hope.
runners.comment: 0-15 hurdling but penultimate Naas third (2m, soft to heavy) offers hope; reserve

runners.name: The Toothpicker
runners.spotlight: Has posted a couple of encouraging efforts since returning from a long absence, notably when fifth of 24 at Cork (2m4f, soft to heavy); not discredited at same venue on latest over 2m; possibly wants further than this nowadays.
runners.comment: Best recent effort over 2m4f, this trip looks on the short side nowadays

runners.name: Midnight Eagle
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; won this last year off 2lb lower (good); standout run this term when second at Thurles on decent ground; soft ground against her at Clonmel last time and likely again so here.
runners.comment: Last year's winner off 2lb lower but wants better ground; reserve

runners.name: Springboard
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in first three handicap starts on decent ground at 2m-2m6f in 2024; returned from 535-day absence when (backed) remote fourth in C&D novice handicap in February; better for that but still best watched on this sort of ground.
runners.comment: Better for C&D comeback run in February but still best watched on this sort of ground

runners.name: Houndsworth
runners.spotlight: Ordinary Flat form in Britain and three quiet runs in maidens before much-improved on handicap debut (following wind surgery and with first-time tongue-tie) at Cork early this month when a 40-1 shot, hitting last but winning going away over 2m (yielding); 10lb rise for that but could go well.
runners.comment: Won going away at Cork; 10lb rise for that but could go well

runners.name: Gaoithe Glory
runners.spotlight: Second in a Listowel bumper (soft) in 2024 on debut; best hurdles run when fifth (beaten 6.5l) in a handicap at same venue last September; absent since below-par run here at Christmas, needs to find more.
runners.comment: Absent since below-par run here at Christmas, needs to find more

runners.name: Liss Eile
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; some decent bumper runs and best of three maiden hurdle efforts came on debut at Tramore last summer; too keen and didn't get home on soft on recent handicap debut at Clonmel; may want better ground.
runners.comment: Didn't get home on soft on recent handicap debut; may want better ground; reserve

runners.name: The Dancen Kid
runners.spotlight: Went closest when third in a Leopardstown handicap over 2m2f last month on heavy; tame Wexford effort since back at 2m so needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Penultimate Leopardstown effort gives him claims, needs to bounce back however

runners.name: Only A Dollar
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 2m Clonmel mares' maiden in 2024; just bits and pieces of form last year, placed at Wexford and prospects when falling two out at Clonmel; absent since September and likely best watched on ground plenty soft for her.
runners.comment: Absent since September and likely best watched on ground plenty soft for her

runners.name: Teagarden Jazz
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden yet to show anything for current yard (formerly with Gordon Elliott); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything for current yard (formerly with Gordon Elliott); hard to fancy

runners.name: Uptaurintop
runners.spotlight: Much-improved when going close over 2m3f here on handicap debut at Christmas; hasn't run anywhere near that level twice since however, not looking to stay 2m5f here last time; cheekpieces now added and top jockey takes over.
runners.comment: Close third here at Christmas gives him claims; cheekpieces added and Kennedy takes over

runners.name: Avandra
runners.spotlight: Moderate sort now 0-16 hurdling, continues to struggle off this basement mark.
runners.comment: Moderate sort now 0-16 hurdling, continues to struggle off this basement mark

runners.name: Comanche Moon
runners.spotlight: Now 0-8 hurdling, last four defeats in handicaps; down 3lb from opening mark but needs to improve a good bit to play a major role.
runners.comment: Down 3lb from opening mark but needs to improve a good bit to play a major role

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Low-grade Flat handicapper nowadays, surprising favourite when well held at Gowran last week; 0-5 hurdling, well held both handicap attempts.
runners.comment: Low-grade Flat handicapper, surprising favourite when well held at Gowran last week

runners.name: Mudslide Molly
runners.spotlight: Not much to take from maidens and soundly beaten on handicap debut over C&D in February when tried in a tongue-tie; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on handicap debut over C&D in February when tongue-tied; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Coup d'Essay
runners.spotlight: No impact so far in maidens; opening mark looks on the high side but yard's handicap debutants always respected.
runners.comment: Opening mark looks on the high side but yard's handicap debutants always respected

runners.name: Out Moll's Gap
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in all three maiden hurdles last season and pulled up at Cork the last twice when big odds; impossible to be confident in.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Cork the last twice; impossible to be confident in

runners.name: Thirsty Work
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo has been well beaten so far in maidens at 2m-2m4f on soft and heavy ground; new headgear combination on for handicap debut; yard among the winners recently.
runners.comment: Nothing much in maidens; new headgear combination on for handicap debut

races.race_name: Professor Maria Hinfelaar Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Classy Cc
runners.spotlight: Maiden has placed in two of her last four starts, including when third in 2m mares' handicap here on heavy in November off 97; good second of 17 at Naas on penultimate but failed to see out 21.5f here 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Good third here over 2m in November and Naas run full of promise; didn't stay last time

runners.name: Fenian Maxios
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in first two starts over around 2m4f before much-improved second to 120-rated rival at Thurles (16.5f, soft to heavy); the form of that race is questionable and he really struggled at Cork ten days ago; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Possibly flattered by his Thurles second; market can guide on his handicap debut

runners.name: Poppycon
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in points without winning; well beaten in three maiden hurdles; runner-up on handicap debut at Wexford last month (2m, soft); step up in trip likely to suit from 2lb higher; contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up on handicap debut at Wexford; step up in trip likely to suit from 2lb higher

runners.name: Brother Hollie
runners.spotlight: The best of three maiden hurdle runs came at Navan on second start; always detached on handicap debut at Down Royal on Sunday; needs more time.
runners.comment: Always detached on handicap debut at Down Royal on Sunday; needs more time

runners.name: Hakensack
runners.spotlight: Disappointed at Thurles on final run for James Hannon but definite player judged on C&D second (soft to heavy) off 78 in December; first run for Philip Rothwell; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Definite player judged on C&D second off 78 in December; first run for Philip Rothwell
runners.owner: Mrs J E Rothwell/Tipp Of Clare Syndicate/L Behan

runners.name: Carlas Big Jim
runners.spotlight: Dual-winning hurdler has largely struggled this term and was pushed along vigorously over fences on latest at Clonmel; Jack Kennedy has been booked though, so he's worth a market check.
runners.comment: Dual-winning hurdler has struggled this term but the booking of Kennedy perks interest

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Has hit the frame four times over fences this season, not beaten far in pair of 23.5f handicaps at Clonmel of late; won Thurles handicap hurdle (soft) over this trip in February last year under this rider off 97; looks set to run a good race again.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat chasing this term; 1lb lower than Thurles hurdles win; solid shout

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Winner of a novice hunter chase at Tipperary last spring (23.5f, good to yielding) on yard debut; 0-6 over hurdles for Noel Meade prior to that; midfield in Clonmel handicap chase two weeks ago (23.5f); others preferred.
runners.comment: Novice hunter chase winner last spring on decent ground; chase campaign; others preferred

runners.name: Port Rashid
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner; losing run stretches back three years but ran a fair race from 10lb out of the handicap when seventh of 17 at Thurles in February; this is still a big ask for a 15yo.
runners.comment: Big run from 10lb out of the weights last time; this is still a big ask for a 15yo

runners.name: Lispopple
runners.spotlight: Won at Roscommon in September 2024 off 3lb lower; only seen four times since, pulled up twice here, tailed off at Clonmel and fell at the second at Cork last month; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Won at Roscommon in September 2024 off 3lb lower; only seen four times since; watch

runners.name: Cooltubrid Eva
runners.spotlight: Went close behind a smart type here on debut in 2023 but has failed to back that up; pulled up in 21.5f course handicap 16 days ago; mark sliding but she's hard to fancy on recent showings.
runners.comment: Went close here on debut in 2023; mark sliding but she's hard to fancy on recent showings

runners.name: Let Him B
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much last season but back from 370-day break when taking advantage of career-low mark at Down Royal last month (20.5f, yielding); the runner-up won well here next time; 11lb higher tackling more testing conditions but can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Back from absence when winning at Down Royal; the second won well here since; 11lb higher

runners.name: Uncle Gerhard
runners.spotlight: Veteran's sole win came at Gowran in January 2022 for Conor O'Dwyer; signs of life this season, especially on latest when runner-up of 16 at Clonmel (2m3f, soft); each-way possibilities from the same mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up of 16 at Clonmel (2m3f, soft); each-way possibilities from the same mark

runners.name: Can't Catch Molly
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in three of her four outings to date, including on handicap debut at Naas (2m); first try at this sort of trip and her pedigree suggests she could improve for it.
runners.comment: First try at this sort of trip and her pedigree suggests she could improve for it

runners.name: Potters Pal
runners.spotlight: Modest form in points; well held in three maidens; landed a sizeable gamble on his third handicap outing at Clonmel three weeks ago (2m3f, soft), although it was a messy race and was left in the lead two out; respected from 8lb higher.
runners.comment: Landed a sizeable gamble at Clonmel three weeks ago, but a messy race; 8lb higher

runners.name: Knockbrack Rambler
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 94 in December 2024; enjoys the mud; jumping issues evident in five poor chase attempts this season; needs to do better but a return to hurdling should help.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 94 in December 2024; needs to do better but a return to hurdling can help

runners.name: Brave Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Point winner on heavy has returned from long layoff this year; beaten 24l on recent handicap debut; completely tailed off at Cork ten days ago; only positive to note is the booking of Darragh O'Keeffe.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off at Cork 10 days ago; only positive to note is booking of O'Keeffe

runners.name: Loi Des Jeux
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in maiden hurdles at 2m-2m5f on varying ground; needs to step up from handicap debut at Punchestown in January but was beaten only 8.5l and this is a slight ease in grade; could improve.
runners.comment: Needs to step up from handicap debut at Punchestown in January but was beaten only 8.5l

races.race_name: Professor Maria Hinfelaar Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Crafty Gael
runners.spotlight: No win in 13 starts, but has gone close on several occasions (placed five times); came back from 800 days off the track when last of 13 at this course last month (2m, soft); won't be suited by heavy conditions.
runners.comment: Came back from 800 days off the track when last of 13 at this course last month

runners.name: Minority Interest
runners.spotlight: Win came at Gowran (2m4f, soft) in October off 86; safely held next two starts but fair run here over an inadequate 2m last time when 4.75l fifth of 13, best work late on; merits consideration back up in trip.
runners.comment: Win was over 2m4f; good run here over inadequate trip last time; considered up in trip

runners.name: Mickey Hulie
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner; confirmed his liking for a stamina test when second over 3m at Naas (soft to heavy) last month; below that level when remote third of 15 here (2m7f, heavy; favourite) eight days later; that perhaps came too soon but he may want further than this; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Relished a stamina test over 3m at Naas; race at this course lto probably came too soon

runners.name: The Little Yank
runners.spotlight: Veteran improved his hurdles strike-rate to 5-60 with Clonmel victory on the 101st outing of his career in January off 93; generally unpredictable; pulled up on his three most recent attempts.
runners.comment: 5-63 over hurdles; generally unpredictable; pulled up on his three most recent attempts

runners.name: Orlando Spirit
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maidens in 2024 for Declan Queally; tailed off at Naas in January; much-improved second of 17 at Thurles in February, staying on strongly to be beaten a neck (16.5f, soft to heavy); raised 8lb at Cork, got going too late to take third on yielding; this trip will suit.
runners.comment: Hit line hard at Thurles before lesser third at Cork on better ground; trip/ground suits

runners.name: Diamond Island
runners.spotlight: Well exposed in points; weak form in maiden hurdles; reached the money on handicap debut over 2m5f at this track but was beaten 27l in third; pulled up at Clonmel last time; new headgear combination; others preferred.
runners.comment: Reached the money on handicap debut at this track but was beaten 27l; pulled up last time

runners.name: Wise Man
runners.spotlight: Placed in two small-field points; set some stiff tasks in maiden hurdles; stuck to his task well on handicap debut at Clonmel (2m, soft) a fortnight ago when beaten 3.5l into fifth of 13; bred to get much further so must be shortlisted.
runners.comment: Eyecatching handicap debut at Clonmel over 2m; bred to get much further; shortlisted

runners.name: Falcon Park
runners.spotlight: Had been struggling to find his best form since joining this yard before an encouraging second behind a well handicapped winner at Clonmel in February; failed to build on that in two subsequent starts.
runners.comment: Player if reproducing Clonmel second in February; not at his best in two starts since

runners.name: Shanroe Nelson
runners.spotlight: Moderate form overall; his 9l third of 16 at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) in January was a decent effort; had excuse for poor chase run there next time; distant fifth here over hurdles on latest (2m7f); place chance.
runners.comment: 9l third of 16 at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) in January was a decent effort

runners.name: Ask The Bookie
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 19 starts; well beaten in C&D novice in December and no better when upped to 3m at Cork; struggled off this mark on last handicap start; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden after 19 starts; struggled off this mark on last handicap start

runners.name: Opposite Ends
runners.spotlight: Has a very patchy profile but would be a player judged on his penultimate third at Clonmel (16.5f, heavy); looked held when brought down two out at that track three weeks ago; market will reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Has a very patchy profile but would be a player judged on his penultimate third at Clonmel

runners.name: Rebel Cowgirl
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt at Punchestown last month (2m4f, soft), making all and scoring by 0.5l; the runner-up, third and fifth have all won since; 8lb higher but should still make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Made all at Punchestown; the runner-up, third and fifth have all won since; bold bid again

runners.name: Ballingurteen
runners.spotlight: In good hands but has shown next to nothing over hurdles since second in a maiden point over a year ago.
runners.comment: In good hands but has shown next to nothing over hurdles since second in a maiden point

runners.name: Boola Times
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best run was when third in Gowran handicap in October (2m4f, yielding); better than bare result at Fairyhouse (2m4f) in December, headway to lead after three out but could find no more in closing stages; could go well after a break.
runners.comment: Chance judged on Gowran third; better than bare result at Fairyhouse; could go well

runners.name: Le Fanu
runners.spotlight: Best form at this venue, third here in November (2m, heavy); beaten 29l in fifth here in February; plenty to prove now.
runners.comment: Best form at this venue, 3rd here in November (2m, heavy); beaten 29l in 5th here in Feb'

runners.name: Gerrymandering
runners.spotlight: Very modest form in maidens at huge prices; could be a different proposition now tackling handicaps; worth monitoring in the betting.
runners.comment: Could be a different proposition now tackling handicaps; worth monitoring in the betting

runners.name: Lizzymac
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in four maiden runs over the winter; stable had first winner since November 2024 at Cork this month so is worth a market check on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Yard had first winner since November 2024 this month; worth a market check on handicap bow

runners.name: Muckanagh Lough
runners.spotlight: Modest point-to-point form but considerably improved on second bumper start for Brian McMahon when good second at Leopardstown over Christmas in steadily-run 2m4f contest (yielding); winner won a maiden hurdle next time; switched yards since and has to be considered a major player.
runners.comment: Leopardstown second has switched yards since; has to be considered a major player

runners.name: Too Hot To Trot
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Rexem (bumper and useful 2m4f-2m7f hurdler/chaser), Geraldo (bumper) and Locken Lady (point); dam unraced sister to bumper/2m hurdle winners Striking Article and Raichu; just denied in Ballindenisk point-to-point on debut for Ciaran Fennessy in November and has joined top yard since; one to consider, especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Just denied in a point-to-point; joined top yard since so respected

runners.name: Double Dare
runners.spotlight: Weakened in straight when remote third on Gowran debut last month (2m2f, heavy); may come on for that so considered.
runners.comment: Recent debut Gowran third; more needed

runners.name: Ruled By The Sun
runners.spotlight: Court Cave 5yo cost 6,500euros as a foal; fifth foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Cloth Fair (2m hurdle), Millrock Lady (2m6f hurdle) and Askmeroe (2m hurdle/2m1f chase); market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Court Cave 5yo cost 6,500euros as a foal; market best guide on debut

runners.name: Sparkling Wit
runners.spotlight: Maxios gelding cost 46,000euros as a foal; brother to top-class 2m-3m2f hurdle/chaser (inc this year's Cheltenham Gold Cup) winner Gaelic Warrior; dam maiden half-sister to 1m2f/10.5f Listed winner Three Bodies; top yard, ultimate respect on debut.
runners.comment: Brother to Gaelic Warrior; ultimate respect on debut for top yard

runners.name: Cloghoula More
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Strike Back (bumper and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase) and Beef To The Heels (2m hurdle); always trailing on recent Cork debut; easily overlooked now.
runners.comment: Always trailing on recent Cork debut; easily overlooked now

runners.name: Little Ginger
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to five-time winner Dromore Lad (stayed well) and bumper scorer Sun Rising Hill; dam unraced sister to a chase winner; always last of four on Punchestown debut in February; best watched.
runners.comment: Always last of four on Punchestown debut in February; best watched

runners.name: Suresuresure
runners.spotlight: Three bumper runs for Richard O'Brien were ordinary enough; needs to find a lot more.
runners.comment: Three bumper runs for Richard O'Brien were ordinary enough; needs to find a lot more

runners.name: Big Boy Dan
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m4f hurdle/chase winner Exit To The West; dam point winner, half-sister to useful 2m7f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Potters Cross; never sighted when well held on belated debut early this month in Fairyhouse winners' bumper; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Well held on belated debut early this month in Fairyhouse winners' bumper; best watched

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Untamed
runners.spotlight: Weak in the betting but still ran well when runner-up at 13-2 on debut at Chelmsford (6f, AW) three weeks ago; his dam was a useful 6f winner and he could have plenty more to offer; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Runner-up on debut on AW three weeks ago; could have plenty more to offer

runners.name: Mind Over Matter
runners.spotlight: Good second on debut at Ayr (6f, good) last June; two lesser runs followed last year but he's from a useful sprinting family and is a leading contender if back to his best in a first-time hood, following a break.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on debut last June; two lesser runs followed but leading claims if back to best

runners.name: Different Opinions
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) last March then failed to build on it when fourth at Southwell (7f, AW); however, he's in top hands and his pedigree suggests the drop in trip will suit; wears first-time blinkers and he remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Has shown promise for top yard and the drop in trip could suit on his third start

runners.name: Tickhill Flyer
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; Tasleet half-brother to winners Akeed Dubawi (1m AW; RPR 80), Tarnhelm (6f-1m; 72) and Billy McGarry (7f-8.6f; 66); dam 5f 2yo winner (90); sole newcomer but interesting to see how he goes in the betting.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners; sole newcomer; interesting to see how he goes in betting

runners.name: Milford Grange
runners.spotlight: Fared best of the newcomers when promising third at Redcar (6f, good) last October; far too soon to write him off but he was disappointing at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) nine days ago, even allowing for a wide trip.
runners.comment: Promising third on sole 2yo start but disappointing at Pontefract nine days ago

runners.name: Vega Storm
runners.spotlight: 14-1, recovered from slow start to finish third of 11 on debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) four weeks ago; it's possible a longer trip will be preference but he could be sharper for that initial outing and is respected.
runners.comment: Eyecatching third on debut at Newcastle last month and could be sharper for that outing

runners.name: Agric Ranger
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal whose unraced dam is from a speedy family; improvement is possible on this second start but he was soundly beaten at 16-1 at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) on his recent debut and needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Improvement possible but soundly beaten at 16-1 on his recent debut at Pontefract

runners.name: Kode Grey
runners.spotlight: Down the field at big odds this year on his first two starts (6f/7f, AW) and considerable improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Down the field at big odds this year on his first two starts (6f/7f, AW)

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Urchin
runners.spotlight: Having been outpaced he kept on for a promising third at 8-1 on debut here (1m, good to firm) last August; gelded since; his pedigree suggests that he will relish this step up in trip on his reappearance; strong contender.
runners.comment: Third over 1m here on sole 2yo start; pedigree suggests that he will relish this trip

runners.name: Poppeye
runners.spotlight: 4yo who made belated debut at Southwell (1m4f, AW) 11 days ago and was beaten 7l in fourth of nine; improvement is necessary but he's a half-brother to the yard's useful 1m2f-1m4f winner Real Terms and may well be able to take a step forward; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 4yo who was fourth on AW on recent belated debut; could take step forward; not ruled out

runners.name: Picaro
runners.spotlight: £10,000 yearling by Havana Gold; first foal; dam French 11.7f winner, half-sister to French 6.5f/7f winner Zalari, out of 1m Listed winner; Newmarket trainer is 2-10 with his Ripon runners; could have a part to play on debut, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: First foal; dam French 11.7f winner; could have a part to play on debut; watch the betting

runners.name: Sea And Sun
runners.spotlight: 33,000gns yearling; third foal; dam 1m/8.6f AW winner (RPR 99), half-sister to useful winners Spangler (7f/1m including 2yo) and Kipps (1m2f 2yo); this Sea The Moon colt could give a good account on debut.
runners.comment: 33,000gns yearling by Sea The Moon; could give a good account on debut

runners.name: Sun Lord
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Almanzor brother to French 1m2f 2yo winner The Sheriff, closely related to 1m2f-1m4f winner Wootton'sun (RPR 101); dam French 1m4f Flat/2m1f hurdle winner from classy family; this newcomer is one to consider.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree and this Almanzor newcomer is one to consider

runners.name: N'Ion
runners.spotlight: There's potential in his pedigree but he was well beaten at big odds last month on his first two starts (1m4f/9.4f, AW).
runners.comment: There's potential in his pedigree but well beaten at big odds last month on first two runs

runners.name: Rocky River
runners.spotlight: Pedigree indicates that the step up in trip will be in his favour but he was reluctant to load and always behind when 40-1 on debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in February; may be best watched for now.
runners.comment: Tackles different trip on second start but always behind at 40-1 on debut in February (1m)

runners.name: Empress Olivia
runners.spotlight: Always prominent when winning a C&D maiden last August; ran her best race in handicaps when second of ten in a 5f event at Catterick (good) last week, keeping on; she will have the stands' rail to help this time and should go well despite stepping up in class.
runners.comment: C&D winner; good second on her reappearance last week; well drawn to attack

runners.name: Mighty Magnus
runners.spotlight: Easy front-running win at Ayr (5.5f, good) last July on his third start; showed promise in nurseries afterwards; gelded during his six-month absence; personal best needed but it is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Made all in Ayr maiden last July (5.5f, good); fair nursery form afterwards; been gelded

runners.name: Wyle Cop
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo and he only had to match that form to make a winning seasonal return at Wolverhampton last month; well backed back in handicap company at Southwell three weeks ago but he was ultimately a shade disappointing; connections now reach for headgear.
runners.comment: AW maiden win on return but couldn't build on it at Southwell last month; headgear on now

runners.name: Yy Spirit
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt when landing a modest fillies' novice (6f) at Newcastle four months ago; sold out of Ed Bethell's for 11,000gns in February; opening mark looks stiff.
runners.comment: Won a 6f AW novice for Ed Bethell in December; sold 11,000gns since; mark looks tough

runners.name: Bye Law
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden/novice events last August (good/good to firm); unplaced in three nurseries afterwards but two of them were on soft ground and the other was a sound effort at York where 5f looked an inadequate test; gelded since last seen; returns on a reduced mark and one of two to consider for a stable with a fine record in the race.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins in maiden/novice company; excuses for three nursery defeats; not discounted

runners.name: Rogue Temptation
runners.spotlight: Won a Haydock novice on her second start and bumped into a useful opponent over C&D last August; ran well on her handicap debut at Doncaster in September but she ended her 2yo campaign with two lesser efforts; could resume here progress this year and she returns with her stable in good form; cheekpieces make their debut (wore blinkers final 2yo run).
runners.comment: Had some useful 2yo form, including C&D; returns with her yard going well; new headgear

runners.name: Cotai Belle
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer, a 5.5f maiden at Wetherby (good to firm) and a 5f nursery at Haydock (good), before coming up short in two sales races and the Listed Two-Year-Old Trophy; still has time on her side but not obviously well handicapped back from 194 days off.
runners.comment: Two wins last summer before coming up short in good company; mark looks tough enough

runners.name: Head For Freedom
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f claimer at Beverley on his second start (last May) and he hit the frame in his first three nursery runs; failed to fire on the slowest ground he had tackled when last seen; needs to have improved over the winter if he is to make a winning return.
runners.comment: Ran okay in his first three nurseries but he needs to have improved over the winter

runners.name: Generous Rascal
runners.spotlight: Green on 2yo debut but took a big step forward when winning a 6f novice at Kempton on his second start (well backed); stiff task next time and his subsequent Southwell third (7f) was a solid start to his handicap career; sold out of Oliver Cole's stable for 35,000gns in February and gelded; new connections quick to drop him back to sprint trips and he remains with some potential
runners.comment: 6f AW winner; outstayed over 7f on handicap debut; still has potential for new yard

runners.name: Storm Esme
runners.spotlight: Well backed prior to her winning debut at Newcastle (6f) last September; failed to fire in the Redcar Two-Year-Old Trophy on second start but back on the up when defying a penalty in a Leicester novice in October (6f, good); the form of her two victories hasn't really worked out but she could do no more than win in decisive fashion and it is likely she has more to come; interesting for the stable that took this in 2016, 2019, 2022 and 2024.
runners.comment: 2-3 as a 2yo, looking a nice prospect; her trainer has won this race four times since 2016

runners.name: Hvar
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar RPR in three runs for Ralph Beckett as a 2yo, getting off the mark in a small-field 6f novice at Wolverhampton when last seen in October; sold for 26,000gns soon afterwards; capable of better but such progress will be required to defy this mark.
runners.comment: 6f AW win on final run for R Beckett; sold 26,000gns afterwards; opening mark demands more

runners.name: Fordham Flyer
runners.spotlight: Two promising runs over 5f for this yard last autumn; stepping up to 6f should be in his favour and he is open to improvement but such progress looks essential if he is to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Promise over 5f last autumn; 6f should suit but improvement essential to defy opening mark

runners.name: Wise Crack
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 7f AW novice on his third run as a 2yo; well beaten over a mile when last seen in September; sold out of John Ryan's stable for 10,000gns in February; likely best watched on his stable/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promising 7f AW run for former yard; sold 10,000gns in February; likely best watched

runners.name: Modern Times
