runners.spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in a C&D maiden last June, his second run for Tim Easterby; more to do back in a competitive handicap after 302 days on the sidelines.
runners.comment: 15th time lucky when winning a C&D maiden last June; absent since; returns in a warm race

runners.name: Wild Clary
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name after 13 starts but he ended his 3yo campaign with two fine efforts, notably when chasing home a well-backed sprinter at Haydock in September (6f, good to soft; traffic problems at a crucial stage), and his return to action at Kempton should have blown away any cobwebs; best efforts on flat tracks but he is feasibly weighted if the undulations of Ripon aren't an issue.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but on handy mark and should be sharper with a recent run behind him

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Enthusiastic front-runner who shone for Ruth Carr in 2025, winning five 6f handicaps; fully effective over C&D and he made an encouraging seasonal return at Catterick last week, second of nine having been headed inside the final furlong (6f, good); looks the likeliest leader up the far side if the field split.
runners.comment: Front-runner; shone in 2025 and last week's return to action was a good one; contender

runners.name: Kodiac Thriller
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer; did well for this yard last year, winning at Thirsk in May and running several good races in defeat, notably when best of the stands' rail group in the Great St Wilfrid over C&D; he needs to leave his low-key seasonal return well behind if he is to feature today though.
runners.comment: Did well last year, including over C&D; on a handy mark but his reappearance was low key

runners.name: American Style
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; C&D winner on his seasonal return on this card 12 months ago; often ran well in good handicaps afterwards and although he ended 2025 with a couple of lesser efforts, he is likely to be seen to much better effect this time; should make a bold bid up the stands' rail.
runners.comment: C&D win on this card last year; ended 2025 tamely; likely to be seen to better effect here

runners.name: Fortamour
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has been a regular in this event, winning in 2022 (soft) and 2024 (heavy) and hitting the frame in both 2023 and 2025; ended 2025 campaign with a pair of AW wins but he hasn't been at that level in either run this year; drying ground not ideal but he can never be ruled out completely at this track.
runners.comment: Record in this race reads 1413; drying ground not ideal but can't rule out at this venue

runners.name: It Just Takes Time
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2023 off an 8lb higher mark; all his subsequent wins have come over 7f+ and his return to action at Doncaster was unconvincing; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Low-key return to action last month and may be better suited by 7f+ nowadays

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner down the year and although most of that success has come on AW, he did pick up a big pot on turf when making all in the Great St Wilfrid over C&D last August; ran well from a wide draw at Lingfield on Good Friday; on a dangerous mark but a central draw probably not ideal today.
runners.comment: Made all in big C&D handicap last year off 3lb higher; good efffort latest; can't dismiss

runners.name: Another Investment
runners.spotlight: More modest runs than good ones in 2025 but he did bolt up at Doncaster last June (6f, good to firm) and he is 4lb lower now; needs to leave his reappearance effort well behind him and he has finished down the field in his two visits here.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but there were more bad days than good in 2025; yet to shine here

runners.name: Sixtygeesbaby
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a good second at Newmarket (6f, good), after which he changed hands for 50,000gns; only eighth (of 19) on his stable debut at Doncaster 18 days ago but he wasn't well drawn as things panned out and he ran as though the run would bring him on; remains a sprinter of interest.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; not well drawn on stable/seasonal debut; remains of serious interest

runners.name: Lesley's Boy
runners.spotlight: Won two of his first three starts, including a C&D nursery off 8lb higher, but the handicapper was in command for much of 2025; creditable third to Fortamour at Newcastle when last seen 145 days ago but he has more on his plate in this deeper race.
runners.comment: C&D winner; struggled for much of 2025; returns to action in a deep race

runners.name: Wen Moon
runners.spotlight: Most of his racing in recent seasons has been at 5f and it was over that trip that he ended a losing streak at Musselburgh in November (soft); 6lb rise unlikely to prove beyond him under his optimum conditions (5f on slow ground) but others appeal more in this field.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a win but that was under ideal conditions, namely 5f and slow ground
runners.owner: Mps Racing, D Shapiro & D Clifford

runners.name: Rock Opera
runners.spotlight: Hold-up sprinter and on a losing run of 20; well handicapped as a consequence and should be winning soon; whether it will be today, after 166 days off and at a track where he is yet to run well, is debatable.
runners.comment: Losing run of 20 has seen him drop down the weights; yet to run well in four visits here

runners.name: Almarada Prince
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in 2025 but he has edged down the weights and his two AW runs for Paul Midgley have both been encouraging enough; no issue with the return to turf; type to win races sooner rather than later.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and both runs for new yard have been encouraging enough; not ruled out

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: Made a flying start to his 2025 campaign, his win in a C&D handicap last May completing a hat-trick; added another C&D handicap in August and also ran a fine race in a Class 2 here in September (off this mark); that C&D form makes him an interesting candidate for a stable with two wins in the race since 2019.
runners.comment: Good C&D form in a productive 2025 campaign; has gone well fresh; one to take seriously

runners.name: Roberto Caro
runners.spotlight: Won a Racing League handicap off this mark last summer; two solid AW efforts this year, keeping on strongly for third in a well-contested 6f handicap at Newcastle three weeks ago; each-way shout.
runners.comment: On last winning mark and he has run two solid races on AW this year; each-way shout

runners.name: Sir William
runners.spotlight: Underwhelming fifth of six on last month's stable and seasonal debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW), but he was wide early and may have needed the run; progressive on turf last season for William Haggas and he could tap back into that promise and play a leading role today.
runners.comment: Underwhelming on stable debut but may have needed the run; progressive last season

runners.name: Spoken Truth
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Dermot Weld but this lightly raced 5yo was a promising fourth at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) on his recent stable and seasonal debut; could have more to offer for this yard; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo who was a promising fourth on recent stable debut; on the shortlist

runners.name: Spioradalta
runners.spotlight: Five wins last year (various ground), two of them over C&D, and campaign concluded with good third at Redcar in November; respectable reappearance run at Doncaster recently and could show the benefit of that outing today.
runners.comment: Five wins last year (two C&D); respectable reappearance; could show benefit of that run

runners.name: Pomme Pomme
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won her first five handicaps (8.6f/1m2f) last May/June and campaign concluded with close second of three to a progressive filly at Hamilton (1m1f) in August; no surprise to see a bold bid on her return, provided conditions suit (unproven on slow ground).
runners.comment: Prolific last year after moving into handicaps; no surprise to see a bold bid on return

runners.name: Glistening Nights
runners.spotlight: Down the field on final two runs last season but he may have been ready for a break; on a handy mark based on his form last August and could be a contender for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: On a handy mark on last August's form & could be a contender for last year's winning yard

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Has race-fitness on his side and his reduced mark appeals on last spring's AW wins; however, he hasn't been at the same level across his five AW starts this year and has to raise his game now back on turf.
runners.comment: Below best on AW this year and has to raise his game now back on turf

runners.name: Lunar Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in Listed race and AW handicap on final two runs in November; however, a break may have helped and she's of interest judged on her two efforts last summer (1m1f/8.3f), when shaping as though 1m2f would suit.
runners.comment: Twice well beaten last November but has had a break since; of interest on earlier form

runners.name: Austrian Theory
runners.spotlight: 0-10 last year but runner-up on his reappearance last April (1m, good) and placed off today's mark on his final two outings last autumn (1m2f, good to soft/soft); might not be far away on this return to action.
runners.comment: 0-10 last year but placed a few times, including on last two starts last autumn

runners.name: Raulin
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won on handicap debut at Redcar (1m2f, good to soft) last May and scored twice in a row at Pontefract (1m2f good/good to firm) last summer; unplaced on final run last year and has to prove his current mark is within range, but he might be the type to continue to progress this season.
runners.comment: Three wins last year and he might be the type to continue to progress this season

runners.name: Financer
runners.spotlight: 0-11 last year when not at his best on his final few runs; however, he was runner-up three times last season, including in this race on his reappearance (good), and he's 2lb lower 12 months on; capable of having a big say.
runners.comment: 0-11 last year but runner-up three times, including in this race on his reappearance
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

races.race_name: Racing BetBuilder At Spreadex Sports Handicap

runners.name: Saxon Prince
runners.spotlight: All five runs for Clive Cox on AW, going close in four-runner handicap at Kempton (7f) last month; sold for 24,000gns since; has shaped as though this step up to 1m is worth a go; one to consider on stable/turf debut.
runners.comment: The step up in trip is worth a go on stable debut and he's one to consider
runners.owner: A Scaife, C Scaife, G Scaife

runners.name: Sailor Batt
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after nine starts but he's been runner-up in two handicaps (including on turf) and was a close third at Newcastle (1m, AW) most recently; every chance he'll be thereabouts once more.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden after nine starts but placed a few times, including last month (1m, AW)

runners.name: Norcross Brow
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but has twice finished runner-up (including on turf) and he was denied a clear run when sixth of nine, beaten only about 2l, at Newcastle (1m, AW) last time; the first-time hood could help him settle better; chance.
runners.comment: 0-6 but has shown promise and the first-time hood could help him settle better; chance

runners.name: Walk On Walk On
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability among his six (winless) 2yo starts for Des Donovan; sold for 7,750gns last October and gelded since; however, improvement is needed in first-time cheekpieces if he's to make a winning stable debut.
runners.comment: Showed ability last year but improvement is needed if he's to make a winning stable debut

runners.name: Fickle McSelfish
runners.spotlight: All seven starts on AW; got off the mark at the sixth attempt in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Newcastle (7f) last month, doing it comfortably from the front; third under a penalty at Southwell (7f) seven days later and now another 4lb higher, but he raced wide early last time and may yet to have more to offer in the headgear; chance if seeing out 1m.
runners.comment: Won in good style at Newcastle then raced wide early when third at Southwell

runners.name: Leysthorpe Star
runners.spotlight: He didn't show much in the winter on his first three starts (7f/1m, AW); however, this half-brother to four winners goes handicapping off a basement mark for his in-form yard and is a possible improver; interesting, all the more so if attracting market support.
runners.comment: Half-brother to four winners who goes handicapping off a basement mark for in-form yard

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: 0-7 last year but the only time he wore these blinkers he was beaten just a neck at Redcar (1m, good to soft) in September; gelded since his last run in November; no surprise if he has a part to play on reappearance.
runners.comment: 0-7 last year but went close the only time he wore these blinkers; gelded since last start

runners.name: Singarda
runners.spotlight: Down the field on three of his four 2yo starts, most recently on handicap debut at Beverley (8.4f, soft) in September when last seen; however, he's had wind surgery since and is on an attractive mark judged on his sixth of 16 at York (7f, good) last July; could be a contender.
runners.comment: Just one good run among his four 2yo starts but attractive mark on that York effort
runners.owner: D Bishop, S Bland & T Coyle

runners.name: Ciao Capo
runners.spotlight: He got better with each of his three runs in nurseries last autumn, winning comfortably at Newcastle (1m, AW) in November; gelded since; he needs to transfer his improvement back to turf but is bred to do better still and a 6lb rise might not stop him if he can continue the good work.
runners.comment: Comfortable win on AW when last seen; player if continuing the good work back on turf

runners.name: Gwen's Girl
runners.spotlight: Kept on for second on debut at Carlisle (6f, soft) last September; two lesser runs followed (6f/1m, AW/good) but she retains potential in view of that initial promise, and she's not ruled out on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Second on debut last September; two lesser runs followed but not ruled out on h'cap debut
runners.owner: E Holmes, M Graham And P Day

runners.name: Domination
runners.spotlight: Well-bred sort but he failed to threaten on his three 2yo starts for Clive Cox, the latest his handicap debut at Nottingham (1m, good) in October; sold for 16,000gns later that month; has to raise his game on stable debut.
runners.comment: Failed to threaten across his four 2yo starts; has to raise his game on stable debut

races.race_name: Go Racing In Yorkshire Apprentice Handicap (Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Series)

runners.name: On The River
runners.spotlight: The vast majority of his wins have come on slow ground but the going was good when he was second in this race last year; reappeared with promising fifth of 22 in the Spring Mile at Doncaster and could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Second in this last year and reappeared with promising fifth at Doncaster; in the mix

runners.name: Chesneys Charm
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark in first-time blinkers at York (7f, good) last July and he could improve for last month's respectable comeback at Southwell (7f, AW); however, he does need to build on that and blinkers are left off.
runners.comment: Needs to build on last month's comeback at Southwell and the blinkers are left off
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Runner-up five times last May-August and he's on an attractive mark on that form; however, he went off the boil in the autumn and his long losing run continues; needs to get back on track on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Runner-up five times last May-August but went off the boil in the autumn; reappearance

runners.name: Mr Mistoffelees
runners.spotlight: In good form in blinkers towards the end of last year, winning at Newcastle (1m, AW) in October; however, he has a poor strike-rate and wasn't at his best in cheekpieces at Newcastle (1m, AW) three weeks ago following a break; cheekpieces remain on today.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle last October but in blinkers, not the cheekpieces he wears today

runners.name: Walsingham
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 for the year (1m, good/soft) when winning at Ascot last September; well beaten at Doncaster in October but on heavy ground; this is his first run since and he could still have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: 3-3 last year prior to October heavy-ground defeat; could still have more left in the tank

runners.name: Milteye
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on last month's reappearance in the Spring Mile at Doncaster but may have needed the run and those ridden more patiently came to the fore; he won three times last year (1m1f/1m, soft/good) and it will be no surprise if he leaves that latest performance miles behind.
runners.comment: Won three times last year and could leave his reappearance performance miles behind
runners.owner: Rise One Racing And W A Bethell

runners.name: Good Morning Alex
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated over C&D (good) last April and went very close at Carlisle in June; well handicapped on that form but a long way below his best on AW this year; the return to turf needs to trigger a major revival.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on turf form last April/June but hasn't shone on AW this year

runners.name: Empirestateofmind
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year (1m/1m1f, good/soft); the last of them came in a seller and he was well beaten on last month's reappearance at Doncaster, but that was the competitive Spring Mile and he may have needed the run; won this in 2022 and he's not discounted off a handy mark.
runners.comment: Two wins last year and may have needed reappearance outing in the Spring Mile

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was below par at the end of last season but reappeared last April with a good fourth in this race (good) and picked up three wins subsequently; he's 3lb below his last winning mark and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: 11yo; below par at the end of last season but ran well in this on his comeback last April

runners.name: Cosmos Raj
runners.spotlight: Three wins last May/July at about 1m on various ground and he's back down to his last winning mark; may have been ready for a break towards end of last season but there's a suspicion he'll improve for this comeback run.
runners.comment: Three wins last May/July & is on last winning mark; may improve for this reappearance run

races.race_name: Sigeric Ltd Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Moonlit Potter
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler for Henry Daly in 2024; 0-8 over fences and disappointing in the main; beaten 22l when runner-up last time; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-8 over fences and disappointing in the main; beaten 22l into second last time

runners.name: Maximum Offers
runners.spotlight: Good-ground hurdle winner; jumped better on second run over fences when third on soft ground at Wincanton (3m1f; first-time cheekpieces) and these conditions should be more to his liking; off for three months but should have every chance.
runners.comment: Third on soft ground at Wincanton; these conditions should be more to his liking
runners.owner: L H & A Snowden & J E Snowden

runners.name: Jullou De Grissay
runners.spotlight: Only 3lb higher than the mark he won off over hurdles (2m7f, good) last summer and he's a dual point winner who should make a chaser; below par last time but after quite a break.
runners.comment: Good-ground hurdle winner; dual point winner who should make a chaser
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

races.race_name: Acre Accountancy Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Iamyouare
runners.spotlight: Two races in Ireland and two in Britain, best when third in a maiden at Bangor (2m, soft) in February; handicap debut at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft; 14-1) last time saw her well beaten after making most; down 3lb and could be more interesting at this trip.
runners.comment: Handicap debut (2m, good to soft) last time saw her well beaten after making most

runners.name: Geordies Betty
runners.spotlight: Minor honours in all four starts over hurdles but the 11l defeat when third of seven on handicap debut at Fontwell (2m6f, good to soft) last time showed that improvement is needed.
runners.comment: 11l third of seven at Fontwell (2m6f, good to soft) showed that improvement is needed

runners.name: Brave Move
runners.spotlight: Similar form in this three handicaps, closest finish when coming from the rear for third of 16 at Taunton (3m, good) last time; off 126 days and back down in trip but has to be considered for yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Similar form in three handicaps, third of 16 at Taunton (3m, good) last time; off 126 days

runners.name: Churchman
runners.spotlight: 0-19 over hurdles, which could be the bottom line, but he's been placed on four of his six starts this season, on last two occasions finishing runner-up on good ground over C&D and Stratford's 2m6f; wore cheekpieces on last three starts; looks set to be seriously involved again.
runners.comment: 0-19 over hurdles but placed in four of six starts this season, runner-up in the last two

runners.name: Tara Iti
runners.spotlight: Eight wins over hurdles for Gary Moore or James Owen but none beyond about 2m1f and, having changed yards in the autumn, he hasn't made an impact for current stable on Flat or over hurdles; down the weights.
runners.comment: He hasn't made an impact for current stable on Flat or over hurdles; down the weights

runners.name: Maestro Du Mesnil
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on all three starts, last time second of six when favourite on handicap debut at Ffos Las (2m, heavy); there's plenty of scope to improve his jumping and to make progress over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Favourite, 2nd of six in handicap at Ffos Las (2m, heavy); scope for better (jumping/trip)

runners.name: Shesupincourt
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles in 2023 (about 2m7f) and once over fences (2m4f) in Feburary 2025, all in the mud; beaten at least 20l on all completions this season, she's opposable despite her fall in the weights.
runners.comment: Beaten at least 20l on all starts this term; opposable despite her fall in the weights

runners.name: Chosen Bae
runners.spotlight: Won handicap debut at Bangor (2m3f, soft) in February 2025 but safely held on all seven starts this season; chasing debut latest; 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Well held since winning on handicap debut in February 2025; 4lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Crystal Days
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner; 0-4 as a hurdler but placed on two occasions and was highly tried last time (after wind surgery); respected at this level.
runners.comment: 0-4 as a hurdler but placed on two occasions and was highly tried last time
runners.owner: Mrs S Lee And E Lee

runners.name: Fidendum
runners.spotlight: Last seen finishing runner-up in a 2m novice at Market Rasen in March 2025 and that form has worked out tremendously well, as the horses he divided have won high-profile handicaps during the last fortnight; market confidence would be welcomed after so long out.
runners.comment: Latest second reads very well; market confidence would be welcomed after so long out

runners.name: Arctic Voyage
runners.spotlight: 6f winner for Ralph Beckett; placed over hurdles for previous yard but pulled up on debut for this one when tried in a hood (now left off); others preferred.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles for previous yard but pulled up on debut for this one

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: High-mileage three-time Flat winner who is now limited in that scene, only ten days ago finishing well held off a basement mark on the AW.
runners.comment: High-mileage three-time Flat winner who is now limited in that scene

runners.name: American Empire
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; placed form over hurdles and he's had wind surgery since a poor run at Fakenham in October; has to be considered in this modest a race.
runners.comment: Placed form over hurdles and he's had wind surgery since a poor run at Fakenham in October

runners.name: Backer Bilk
runners.spotlight: 1m2f winner 14 months ago; has always operated at a modest level and likely best watched on this hurdle debut.
runners.comment: 1m2f winner 14 months ago; has always operated at a modest level

runners.name: Hellion
runners.spotlight: Finished third in two Flat races for Brian Meehan; has shown nothing so far over hurdles; had a wind op.
runners.comment: Third in two Flat races for Brian Meehan; shown nothing so far over hurdles; had wind op

runners.name: Gallant Lion
runners.spotlight: Some notable Flat form last year, in victory and defeat; two runs over hurdles last February were underwhelming but raw ability is respected.
runners.comment: Two runs over hurdles last February were underwhelming but Flat ability is respected

runners.name: Hopjes
runners.spotlight: Two non-completions over hurdles; placed on the AW last month but over 1m and at a very modest level.
runners.comment: Two non-completions over hurdles; placed on the AW last month but at a very modest level

runners.name: Bowling Shane
runners.spotlight: Limited in four Flat runs and tailed off in both his runs over hurdles.
runners.comment: Limited in four Flat runs and tailed off in both his runs over hurdles

runners.name: Dinkys Rose
runners.spotlight: Beaten 49l on bumper debut and 29l in a maiden hurdle at Ludlow.
runners.comment: Beaten 49l on bumper debut and 29l in a maiden hurdle at Ludlow

runners.name: Good For You
runners.spotlight: None too consistent thus far over hurdles and now 0-7; each-way chance on the pick of his efforts but others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-7; each-way chance on the pick of his efforts but others preferred

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Had one run over hurdles, finishing tailed off in a maiden in late 2024; multiple Flat winner since December and wasn't beaten far at Pontefract last week (1m; stays 1m4f); should run well if jumping stands the test.
runners.comment: Running well enough on Flat to believe he can have a say on belated second run over jumps

runners.name: Sherborne
runners.spotlight: Easily defied a 7lb rise for his Exeter win when strolling clear at Ascot (2m5f, good), albeit in a race that completely fell apart in behind; unlikely that this will be so straightforward off 8lb higher but arrives with his tail up.
runners.comment: The Ascot race fell apart and he's 8lb higher; does arrive with his tail up, however

runners.name: Insurrection
runners.spotlight: Back to his winning trip over fences after failing to defy some serious stamina doubts over the 3m2f of the Kim Muir; he's none too consistent and looked a hard ride at Musselburgh the run before (2m4f), but he's realistically handicapped if he can travel kinder in the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 3m2f too far last time; can look a hard ride so the cheekpieces could be significant

runners.name: Coastguard Station
runners.spotlight: Enjoys good ground on which he beat the useful Mahons Glory by 8l at Market Rasen in September (2m5f); tailed off since then on either side of a 140-day break but those were strong handicaps; respected.
runners.comment: Likes good ground; back at the right level after tough tasks at Ascot and Newbury

runners.name: Black Hawk Eagle
runners.spotlight: 3-13 over hurdles; only his fourth start as a chaser when too strong for two rivals over 2m at Chepstow a month ago (soft; 3lb lower); this quicker ground won't bother him at all and neither might the longer trip; good claims.
runners.comment: Only his fourth start as a chaser when too strong for two rivals over 2m at Chepstow

runners.name: Numitor
runners.spotlight: Potentially very well handicapped but has become a law unto himself and is wickedly hard to predict.
runners.comment: Very well handicapped but has become a law unto himself and is wickedly hard to predict
runners.owner: Paul G Jacobs And Wetumpka Racing

runners.name: Court In A Storm
runners.spotlight: Good ground for all seven of his races; won in small fields for a maiden (here) and a novice hurdle; ran creditably in 3m/2m7f handicaps on last two outings, seemingly needing some respite from the handicapper, who's not made much of a reaction, but this field may offer him a better chance.
runners.comment: Good ground for all seven races; ran creditably in 3m/2m7f handicaps on last two outings

runners.name: Gold For Alec
runners.spotlight: Both wins were at Huntingdon in winter 2024-25; ran well at Cheltenham (2m) in October but has had a bleak time of it since until something of an upturn at Chepstow (extended 2m3f) two weeks ago; needs to build on that and there was some encouragement last time for stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: Bleak spell before something of an upturn at Chepstow (extended 2m3f) two weeks ago

runners.name: Grangeclare Diego
runners.spotlight: 2-8 in points and 1-5 under rules; took a handicap at Market Rasen (2m7f, good; 2lb higher today) in March 2025 but heavy defeats in his only two runs since; at least he could be back on good ground today; trainer had a winner 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Won at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) in March 2025; heavy defeats in his only two runs since

runners.name: Queens Venture
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Dan Skelton with two wins last summer (about 2m6f); ran well for a long way on stable debut at Newcastle in November but pulled up at Chepstow three weeks later, both at about 3m; not proven over this far and absent 131 days but she's won twice after breaks of about three months; trainer had a winner nine days ago.
runners.comment: Not proven over this far and absent 131 days but she's won twice before after breaks

runners.name: Block Rockin Beats
runners.spotlight: 1-8 over hurdles, up in trip when winning at Lingfield (2m7f, soft) in December; with two major backward steps since, a lot seemingly depends on his subsequent wind surgery and today's first tongue-tie.
runners.comment: 2m7f win in December; two major backward steps before wind surgery and first tongue-tie

runners.name: I'm A Starman
runners.spotlight: Registered a four-timer last April-June (3m1f/2m7f, good/good to soft); very in and out since and he's now a 13yo but he finished on the heels of the principals off today's mark at Warwick in November when last seen; more to prove after the break.
runners.comment: Four-timer last April-June; very in and out since and he's now a 13yo; off since November

runners.name: Diyaken
runners.spotlight: Debut winner in France and runner-up at Ascot on only run for Gary Moore; returning from 1221 days off when finishing right behind Wild Goose at Kempton (2m, good), despite bleeding from the nose; the nagging concern is that the second run back from an absence isn't always as good as the first.
runners.comment: Returning from 1221 days off when finishing right behind Wild Goose at Kempton

runners.name: Wild Goose
runners.spotlight: Went up 12lb for winning so well at Ludlow in December (2m, good to soft; first run in a tongue-tie) but probably bettered that form when runner-up at Kempton (good), finishing just ahead of Diyaken; solid chance with Aamilah Aswat again aboard.
runners.comment: Probably his best run yet when runner-up at Kempton, finishing just ahead of Diyaken

runners.name: Phoenix Risen
runners.spotlight: Fine on all ground; in a good run of form since the blinkers went on four starts back and this might not be quite so demanding as the Exeter race he contested last time; in the mix.
runners.comment: This might not be quite so demanding as the Exeter race he contested last time

runners.name: Fight My Fire
runners.spotlight: Hasn't made any striking progress since winning his maiden in October but it wasn't a bad run off 2lb higher here last time; shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: It wasn't a bad run off 2lb higher here last time; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Sun Art
runners.spotlight: Jumping has been an issue in his two runs over fences; lightly raced hurdler with novice honours to his name and worth considering back in this sphere off what's probably a realistic mark; all headgear left off (has always sported either a hood or tongue-tie).
runners.comment: Jumping has been an issue in his two runs over fences; novice winner in this sphere

runners.name: Red Rubio
runners.spotlight: Won his only point by 4l on good ground; beaten 13l and 11l over hurdles so he's not the easiest to weigh up, but goes handicapping at a modest level here.
runners.comment: He's not the easiest to weigh up, but goes handicapping at a modest level here

runners.name: Grey Skies
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer (2m/2m1f, good), the latest off just a 1lb lower mark; form has dipped in his last two races, at Cheltenham and then Carlisle where he should have run better; absent since November.
runners.comment: Form dipped in last two races, at Cheltenham then Carlisle where he should have run better

runners.name: Benvoy
runners.spotlight: In the reapplied blinkers he beat a fair yardstick at Huntingdon just before Christmas (2m, good to soft) but no shows since then, in a visor and blinkers; looks risky with cheekpieces now turned to.
runners.comment: Hard to predict and another change of headgear is tried; risky

runners.name: The Tide Turns
runners.spotlight: Initially promising over hurdles for Gordon Elliott but lost his way under both codes and left that yard for £1,600 in August 2024; twice pulled up since returning from his long absence.
runners.comment: Left Gordon Elliott cheaply and twice pulled up since returning from his long absence

races.race_name: Wye Valley Metals Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: My Mate AJ
runners.spotlight: Short in the betting for both his runs (Southwell, soft; Lingfield, AW), the latest of which was in January; the fact he hung left on both occasions is worrying given the nature of this track, but no doubting his ability to have a major say.
runners.comment: Hung left-handed in both his races but clearly has the ability to have a major say

runners.name: Oykel Bridge
runners.spotlight: Similar form at Warwick and Haydock (both good to soft) and was keeping on both times; good form claims and he has the scope to keep improving.
runners.comment: Good form claims on Warwick/Haydock runs and he has the scope to keep improving

runners.name: Hercules Honour
runners.spotlight: £32,000 yearling; first foal out of a useful 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner; tongue-tie fitted for debut and market confidence would be welcomed; yard 1-22 in bumpers this season.
runners.comment: £32,000 yearling; first foal out of a useful 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner

runners.name: He's Tailor Made
runners.spotlight: Beaten more than 100l at Chepstow where he raced freely; hood added; limited appeal.
runners.comment: Beaten more than 100l at Chepstow where he raced freely; hood added; limited appeal

runners.name: Sky Grove
runners.spotlight: Clear second in an Irish point in January and the winner has done well this year for Chris Gordon; hood added; possible.
runners.comment: Clear second in an Irish point in January and that form has been franked

runners.name: Tommy Tipa
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 11.6f Flat (RPR 84) and 2m/2m1f hurdle winner; trainer doesn't have many bumper runners but she's won one this season.
runners.comment: First foal; dam 11.6f Flat (RPR 84) and 2m/2m1f hurdle winner

runners.name: Right Royal Rascal
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 3m1f/3m2f hurdle winner, closely related to winners Tarada (2m3f hurdle/3m chase) and Travada (point/bumper and 3m chase); yard has occasional success in bumpers and the market needs checking.
runners.comment: Dam 3m1f/3m2f hurdle winner; yard has occasional success in bumpers

runners.name: The Hostage
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield but a distance behind the winner when 8-1 at Huntingdon (good to soft); had to show more.
runners.comment: Finished midfield but a distance behind the winner when 8-1 at Huntingdon

runners.name: Autumn Air
runners.spotlight: Friendless in the betting and trailed home last at Stratford in early March (soft).
runners.comment: Friendless in the betting and trailed home last at Stratford in early March (soft)

runners.name: Jim Santiago Bowie
runners.spotlight: Inexperience was evident but he finished tailed off at Taunton and went off 66-1.
runners.comment: Inexperience was evident but he finished tailed off at Taunton and went off 66-1

runners.name: Kappa Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Too green to do himself justice when beaten 23l at Southwell (soft; 25-1) and entitled to step up on that form.
runners.comment: Too green to do himself justice when beaten 23l at Southwell (soft; 25-1); can improve

runners.name: Bound For Glory
runners.spotlight: 11-1 when beaten 15l into fourth on soft ground at Catterick in January; more needed but may encounter drier conditions this time.
runners.comment: 11-1 when beaten 15l into fourth on soft ground at Catterick in January

runners.name: Sovereign Spell
runners.spotlight: Made all for comfortable reappearance success in Wolverhampton maiden (6f, AW) that was won last year by Double Rush, who followed up in this race; mark is unaltered for this second attempt at handicap level; interesting given his good credentials and likelihood of further progress.
runners.comment: Gained reappearance win in AW maiden that threw up the winner of this contest last year

runners.name: Albaydaa
runners.spotlight: Made all in Wolverhampton maiden (7f, AW) to take her 2yo record to 321; likely to be as effective back down in distance; looks the type to improve further and do well in handicaps for her major connections; attractively weighted near the bottom of the card and is one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Looks the type to improve further and do well in handicaps for her major connections

runners.name: Treanmor
runners.spotlight: Cost 2,000,000euros as a yearling and impressively justified favouritism over C&D (good to firm) last May on debut; beaten in three races over 7f/1m since but ran well at Meydan latest; gelded since; interesting with progress possible returned to this scenario on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Impressive over C&D last May and looks interesting back in this scenario on handicap debut

runners.name: Silent Applause
runners.spotlight: Absent since coming up short in Listed grade at York Ebor festival when bidding for 5f hat-trick; return to 6f presents a question mark in this competitive field and William Buick prefers Treanmor; however, cost 1,400,000gns at the Craven breeze-up a year ago and can't be written off.
runners.comment: Has a bit to prove back at 6f but can't be written off; William Buick prefers Treanmor

runners.name: Advertised
runners.spotlight: Appeared to run well in valuable prize at York (7f) on final 2yo start but an 11lb rise in his mark doesn't help in this ongoing bid for a first turf success; 2-2 on AW, both performances at Newcastle (6f/7f).
runners.comment: 11lb rise in his mark in this ongoing bid for a first turf win; 2-2 on AW

runners.name: Sayidah Hard Spun
runners.spotlight: Gained her handicap success at a major track (6f) but against only three rivals and faces a tougher scenario in this deeper field; 4lb rise for final effort (fifth in substandard AW Group 3) adds to the difficulty; on a positive note, there is a chance she can still improve further.
runners.comment: Raised 4lb for finishing fifth in substandard AW Group 3 but may do better still

runners.name: Reciprocated
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since disappointing in Ayr nursery in September when back down in class after defeats at Listed/Group level; commenced last season with two wins over 6f, looking a useful sort, and this reappearance may be the time to catch him; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Commenced last season with two wins; this reappearance may be the time to catch him
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Lebron Power
runners.spotlight: Brings very good stats, being 3-6 overall, 2-2 in handicaps (both Class 4) and 2-2 over 6f; prevailed narrowly at Newbury (soft) on last appearance; now 2lb higher in a better race but may have further improvement in her; one to consider.
runners.comment: Brings good stats, being 3-6 overall, 2-2 in handicaps and 2-2 over 6f; one to consider

runners.name: Enricher
runners.spotlight: Required a switch to AW at Wolverhampton (6f maiden) to open his account at the fourth attempt, and needs further improvement to follow up in this warm handicap back on turf; wears first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Needed a switch to AW to open his account; goes into deeper waters back on turf

runners.name: Song Of The Clyde
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in the Two Year Old Trophy at Redcar when last seen but otherwise a progressive sort and may do better still; performed well in sales races at York (won) and Doncaster (close second; form has been strongly franked); capable of playing a big role, even off top weight.
runners.comment: Largely progressive form includes good performances in two sales races; in the mix
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker

runners.name: Ironwill
runners.spotlight: Built on debut effort by completing a September double, showing versatility with the wins over 6f on fast ground and 5f on soft; readily defied a penalty in novice event at Beverley and looks open to further progress this season; possibilities.
runners.comment: Completed a September double; looks open to further progress this term; possibilities

runners.name: Pilu
runners.spotlight: Absent since finishing last of four in Salisbury conditions race when attempting to take record to 3-3; may still have more to offer but RPRs suggest he's facing a stiff task off a mark of 87 in this deep field.
runners.comment: Proved unable to take record to 3-3 and RPRs suggests he's on a stiff mark

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Had an easy task for sole win; good second in sales/Listed races at York, including behind Song Of The Clyde, but form dipped sharply on final 2yo start; has a difficult mark in this first handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Ran well in one of his Listed attempts but has to overcome a difficult mark

runners.name: Sir Alfie
runners.spotlight: Scored twice during his spell with Ger Lyons, then posted duck eggs in two Listed events for Charles Byrnes; gelded since; 1-1 at handicap level but this looks a tougher task on debut for new stable.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; 1-1 at handicap level but this looks a stiffer task on stable debut

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Good seventh of 19 at the York Ebor festival (6f) in sole handicap attempt but is a tad exposed, as record of 0-6 since debut success reflects; below-par third of four in conditions race on final 2yo start.
runners.comment: Tad exposed, as record of 0-6 since debut success reflects; below par last time

runners.name: Rogue Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Reasonably exposed and doesn't look the answer on the balance of his AW form this year; gained his 2yo wins at a lower level; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Returns to turf; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear

races.race_name: Betway Wood Ditton Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crown Knott
runners.spotlight: Already-gelded son of Lope De Vega; third foal; half-brother to 5f winner Mc Loven (including 2yo/AW; RPR 88); dam 6f 2yo winner (81), half-sister to winners Blair House (UAE 1m1f Group 1) and Key Victory (1m2f Listed), out of maiden sister to 1m Group 1 winner Poet's Voice; represents powerful connections who have fielded winners of this race before; key contender.
runners.comment: Already-gelded son of Lope De Vega; powerful connections and he's a key contender

runners.name: Portcullis
runners.spotlight: By Frankel; second foal; half-brother to 1m winner Crown Estate (including AW; RPR 100); dam 1m winner (including AW and French 1,000 Guineas; 112), half-sister to 7f Listed winner Top Score, out of unraced sister to this yard's top-class 7f-1m2f winner Raven's Pass; plenty of blue blood in that pedigree and he's one of two for a stable that won this in 2021 and 2022; of obvious interest on this debut with Ryan Moore booked.
runners.comment: By Frankel out of French 1,000 Guineas winner; of obvious interest with Ryan Moore booked
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: North Beach
runners.spotlight: 390,000euros yearling by New Bay; third foal; half-brother to 1m AW winner Indian Spirit (RPR 86); dam Listed-placed 1m2f winner (96), half-sister to winners Sabbeeh (smart 6f-1m) and Dream Day (useful 6f 2yo), out of 7f Listed winner/1,000 Guineas fourth; the most expensive of these at auction and hails from a leading yard that won this with a well-backed colt last year; betting should prove informative again.
runners.comment: 390,000euros yearling by New Bay; leading yard won this with well-backed colt last year

runners.name: Santushti
runners.spotlight: Dubawi fifth foal; brother to winners Lord Of Love (1m2f/1m4f; RPR 102) and Governor Of India (1m2f AW; 77); dam 1m-10.5f winner (including 2yo/French Oaks; 109), closely related to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner English Ballet, half-sister to 1m Listed winner King Of Comedy; lots to like about that pedigree and it will be interesting to see what the market makes of him, particularly in relation to his top stable's other contender Portcullis; firmly considered with Oisin Murphy up.
runners.comment: By Dubawi out of a French Oaks winner; must be considered with Oisin Murphy on board

runners.name: Me An' All
runners.spotlight: Dream Ahead sixth foal; half-sister to four winners including Ventura Gold (7f-1m1f including AW; RPR 79), Chloe's Courage (7f AW; 56) and Kannon (French 5.5f/7.5f); dam 7f AW winner (76), out of 11.7f winning sister to 7f Group 3 winner Yalaitanee; the sole filly and best watched without good market support.
runners.comment: The sole filly and she's best watched without good market support

runners.name: Bemersyde
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns yearling by Ghaiyyath; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Warnie (6f-1m including 2yo/Australian Group 2; RPR 115), Montpellier Green (7f 2yo; 81) and Mutaraafeq (1m AW; 79); dam French 9.7f AW winner (including 2yo), half-sister to 1m1f Listed winner On Call Now; respected connections and market may help ahead of this introduction.
runners.comment: 120,000gns yearling by Ghaiyyath; market may help ahead of this introduction

runners.name: Cosmo Brown
runners.spotlight: By Ghaiyyath; 34,000euros foal, 25,000gns yearling; second foal; half-brother to German 1m2f winner Poyraz Approach; dam unraced sister to useful winners News At Six (1m2f-1m4f) and Leafy Shade (6f 2yo/8.5f); likely best watched on this first go unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 34,000euros foal, 25,000gns yearling; best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise

runners.name: Dark Whisper
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling by Dark Angel; fourth foal; half-brother to German 1m1f AW winner Bavaria; dam 9.4f-1m4f winner (including AW/Listed; RPR 102), sister to unraced dam of 6f Group 1 winner Shaquille, out of 5f Group 2 winner; mixed messages distance-wise from this gelding's pedigree but he's worth a market check.
runners.comment: 50,000euros yearling; mixed messages distance-wise from this gelding's pedigree

runners.name: Impierious
runners.spotlight: By Palace Pier; tenth foal; closely related to 1m-1m4f winner Mashhoor (including Group 3; RPR 115), half-brother to seven winners including Fambrus (Italian 1m7f Listed; 101) and Faciascura (Italian 1m2f Group 3; 100); dam Italian Group-placed 1m2f/1m3f winner; yard in decent nick but this gelding's claims would be aided by market support.
runners.comment: By Palace Pier; yard in decent nick but gelding's claims would be aided by market support
runners.owner: Hasmonean Racing, S Hope & S Barrow

runners.name: Take A Chill Pill
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling by Kingman; second foal; half-brother to 1m2f AW winner Star Cast (RPR 71); dam Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (including 2yo/AW; 105), is granddaughter of 11.7f winning sister to 7f Group 3 winner Yalaitanee; the only one of these to hold a big-race entry (The Derby) so look for market support to back that up.
runners.comment: 65,000gns yearling by Kingman; holds a Derby entry; look for market support

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who held on by a neck in last year's Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm); had excuses for her subsequent defeats in other major sprints at Goodwood (drop back to 5f; still ran creditably) and Haydock (poorly drawn); has the best chance at the weights.
runners.comment: Won last year's Commonwealth Cup; subsequent defeats can be excused; leading contender
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Beckford's Folly
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to prevailing narrowly in the Cornwallis (5f, good; Group 3) on final 2yo start, taking record on the Newmarket tracks to 3-3; effective over 6f and looks open to further improvement; carries 3lb penalty; respected kept to this venue in first attempt against his elders.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to Cornwallis win, taking record on the Newmarket tracks to 3-3; respected

runners.name: Elmonjed
runners.spotlight: Has form on the Newmarket tracks but recorded last three wins at York, including when beating Crestofdistinction with more in hand than the narrow margin suggests in Listed grade on most recent appearance; likely to be more interesting when back at that venue.
runners.comment: Recorded last three wins at York and may be more interesting when back there

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive, mostly in handicaps, throughout his career; won by 1l going away from Jasour in Listed event at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) on seasonal debut, looking better than ever and taking record to 6-23; solid second in both C&D starts; enters calculations upped further in grade.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; won by 1l going away from Jasour at Doncaster on seasonal debut

runners.name: Prince Of India
runners.spotlight: Only fifth when favourite for AW event on reappearance but is reasonably interesting back on turf, provided he gets ideal ground; has form figures of 12113 on good/firmer, the final effort in Listed grade behind Elmonjed; may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 12113 on good/firmer; interesting provided he gets similar ground

runners.name: Diligent Harry
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Listed sprint double at Lingfield this year, sharing the spoils with Completely Random on reappearance; similar to that rival, looks far from guaranteed to maintain the form back on turf; 0-18 in this sphere.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Lingfield Listed double this year; 0-18 on turf

runners.name: My Mate Alfie
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding whose good spell in 2024 featured a Group 3 win and who ran creditably a few times last year; seemed to need the run at Doncaster on stable/seasonal debut but has work to do to reverse placings with Aramram and Jasour.
runners.comment: Group winner in Ireland; seemed to need the run at Doncaster on debut for new yard

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Very useful front-runner whose strike-rate is 12-32; bagged Group 3 honours at York (7f) last June and a valuable pot at Doha (6f) most recently; unbeaten (4-4) in 6f contests on the Newmarket courses and has shown that he can win in sizeable fields; likely to make a bold bid back here.
runners.comment: Very useful front-runner; unbeaten (4-4) on the Newmarket courses; should make a bold bid

runners.name: Completely Random
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated with Diligent Harry in Listed grade to complete a Lingfield 6f double in January and take AW record to 4-8; effective when fresh but is by no means certain to transfer the improvement back to turf; only 1-9 in this sphere; tongue-tie applied.
runners.comment: Dead-heated with Diligent Harry in Listed race, taking AW record to 4-8; just 1-9 on turf

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 5f last year, bagging Listed honours at York in July prior to facing stiffer tasks returned to Group 1 level; effective at 6f in the past and produced his peak RPR when landing this prize (on good ground) in 2024; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 5f Listed winner in 2025; produced peak RPR when landing this prize in 2024; not ruled out

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Very useful 6f performer who registered last success in Group 3 contest at Ascot two years ago; has Group 1 form, including creditable sixth in the 2025 July Cup; made a solid start to his 2026 campaign with 1l second to Aramram at Doncaster; again likely to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Very useful and has Group 1 form; solid effort behind Aramram at Doncaster on reappearance

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Proved well ahead of his mark in sole handicap attempt and only start over 6f, then finished a good third in the Cornwallis (behind Beckford's Folly) and the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; receives weight all round and could go well.
runners.comment: Good third in the Cornwallis and Breeders' Cup race; 1-1 over 6f; could go well

runners.name: Crestofdistinction
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-5 on turf compared to 2-3 on AW; admittedly registered his peak RPR in this sphere when second Elmonjed in York Listed contest (6f) on penultimate appearance; however, needs an even bigger effort to go one better in this field.
runners.comment: Posted peak RPR on turf but needs an even bigger effort to gain first win in this sphere

runners.name: Run To Freedom
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form, such as placed efforts in two July Cups, but is getting long in the tooth and seems unlikely to break his Abernant duck at the fourth attempt (record in this race reads 404); losing spell goes back almost three years.
runners.comment: Capable of smart form but seems unlikely to break his Abernant duck at the fourth attempt

runners.name: Hawk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Made all and did enough to beat two stablemates in the Futurity at Doncaster (heavy; Group 1) to complete a 1m hat-trick and take record to 3-4; may ideally need further this year, especially when the ground isn't testing; otherwise the form pick and an improving Ballydoyle colt to boot.
runners.comment: Made all in the Futurity to complete a 1m hat-trick; improving colt who is the form pick
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Hidden Force
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who is two from two, namely AW wins at Kempton, last time comfortably beating Venetian Prince in 1m conditions race that his yard landed in 2024 and 2025 with subsequent Group 1 scorers (including 2,000 Guineas); the signs look very good for this highly promising sort.
runners.comment: Two from two, namely AW wins at Kempton, latest in a notable conditions race; respected

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Unsuited by heavy ground in the Futurity on final 2yo start; steadily progressive otherwise, and ran well in the Champagne Stakes at Doncaster and Dewhurst at Newmarket; however, chance of opening his Group account largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of opening his Group account depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Scored at Newbury when last seen on turf; looked flattered to finished just under 2l behind Hidden Force, who had something in hand, at Kempton most recently and is unlikely to reverse the placings; however, bred to do better still and may produce another good effort.
runners.comment: Unlikely to reverse Kempton placings with Hidden Force but again could go well

runners.name: Commander's Intent
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-7 and broadly consistent last term; creditable second in last three starts, including in 7f Group 3 at Newmarket; however, his ceiling is seemingly already known and, unlike all of today's rivals, he doesn't have a 2,000 Guineas entry.
runners.comment: His ceiling is seemingly already known and he doesn't have a Guineas entry

runners.name: Hankelow
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder colt who nearly went 3-3 last season, the sole defeat when nabbed by Avicenna on the line at Doncaster; justified favouritism in the Autumn Stakes over C&D (good to firm; Group 3) next time, making all; progressive and holds solid claims.
runners.comment: Made all in Group 3 over C&D on last appearance; progressive and has solid claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Two from two last year, both starts over 7f at Doncaster, latest in Listed grade (on good ground) when collaring Hankelow on the line despite still seeming green; may have plenty of further progress in him, especially over this extra furlong; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Two from two, both starts at Doncaster; collared Hankelow in Listed grade latest

runners.name: Time For Sandals
